body: Based on experiences for folks who have had bunion surgeries - Who did them? Podiatrist or orthopedic surgeon? I have 2 medical opinions so far. Podiatrist took and x-ray and examined my foot. He recommended lapiplasty (and would have me sign a consent in case he needed to do a 1st MTP arthrodesis). He said I still have movement in my big toe so he wouldn't do the 1st MTP as first course of action. He has years of experience. Orthopedic surgeon who specializes in foot and ankle said she would do the 1st MTP arthrodesis and not the lapiplasty. She said I wouldn't be happy with the results of lapiplasty and would be mad at her afterwards. She has 13 years of experience and said she has done 100s of these. I'm not sure if these differences in recommendations are podiatrist vs. orthopedic surgeon or if they are generational. I'm going do a 3rd opinion this week and will go for a 4th, etc. Thoughts?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Tip That Helped My Post-Bunion Surgery Swelling and Toe Pressure. I discovered something during recovery from bunion fusion and hammer-toe surgery that might help others dealing with severe swelling and pressure in the toes. When my leg was elevated with the foot pointing straight up toward the ceiling, swelling seemed to collect in the forefoot. This caused strong pressure between the toes where the gauze spacer was placed and created a sharp, cutting type of pain. When I rotated my leg slightly so the foot pointed sideways instead of straight up, while keeping the leg elevated, the pressure between the toes dropped significantly. The surgical areas became much calmer and the pain was much more manageable. Elevation was still maintained above heart level. The only thing that changed was the orientation of the foot. My personal observation is that when the toes are the highest point, swelling may collect in the forefoot. Allowing the foot to turn slightly sideways or tilt a little can let swelling redistribute away from the toes. After the pressure decreased, I noticed some mild warming or tingling in the toes, which felt like circulation returning after the swelling reduced. This isn't medical advice — just a recovery observation that helped me when the pressure between my toes became very painful.

body: Has anyone here ever dealt with toenail nail fungus? What treatment actually worked for you?

I've battled with my toenail fungus for 20 years, trying various topical over the counter medications, curanail, scholl....they did not work. I tried soaking them in epsom salts, applying tea tree oil, which did improve them but not destroy the fungus permanently. I tried imperial feet as it was highlighted rated on amazon, that just made the nails soft to cut it back, again not killing the fungus. I then tried laser treatment, which was expensive, but came highly rated to cure toenail fungus. I had high hopes but it did not work. I finally had success in February 2025. I went to my doctors and got prescribed antifungal terbinafine tablets, 1 a day for 3 months, it's taken a few more months for the nails to fully regrow but I'm now toenail fungus free.

body: I'm a healthy 70 yr. old facing Arthrex minimally invasive surgery. I live alone. Will I be able to manage on my own? How soon before I can walk my dog?

Friendly reminder from someone who learned the hard way. If you're scheduled for (or recovering from) minimally invasive bunion surgery, please don't rush the walking even if you feel like you can. I truly thought I was being careful, but starting to walk too soon caused my toe to shift and the screws in my foot to move. Now there's a possibility my doctor may have to go back in and remove them, which was never my intention. I'm sharing this as a gentle warning, not to scare anyone just to encourage rest, patience, and following post-op instructions closely. Healing takes time, even when the surgery is minimally invasive. Learn from my experience: rest really means rest. Your future self (and your foot!) will thank you. Sending healing vibes to everyone in recovery. Side note: I have to wear my surgical shoe for another month and watch the swelling and possible pain. This is not fun but could've been prevented.

comments: Alignment of the foot is dictated by connective tissue not the skeleton. If you debone a fish, it does not change form. The fact that 3 different doctors have 3 different surgical plans, for me, is a red flag.

Have you tried a shoe with a rocker bottom and a carbon plate or really stiff sole that it doesn't bend? I have walked on my little toe for many years and finally went to wide width shoes even though I don't need a wide width and my toes have naturally spread enough.

I had a Taylor's bunion and had surgery on it back in January of this year. During surgery, the surgeon broke my 5th metatarsal, shortened it and added a plate and screws along with a bone graft from my heel. I TOTALLY underestimated the recovery. Foot surgery is no joke.
You might consider doing one of the online foot programs like Gait Happens or Petra Fisher. There is a lot of free content. Wearing minimalist/barefoot shoes has shown me WHERE my gait issues are.
Your feet sound like mine. I use toe separators...you can find various ones on Amazon, and wear Chaco Z sandals and boots. If I wear them most of the time, I can wear other shoes some of the time.

ALL surgery carries risk. And that's the risk you take if anyone messes with your feet. Think long and hard about having surgery. It may make things worse. I've read far too many post-surgery foot horror stories.

I have a new diagnosis for my ongoing metatarsal pain - Morton's neuroma. According to my new doctor yesterday's MRI shows no evidence there was ever stress reactions/fracture as was my prior diagnosis.

body: Had my one week follow up today! The left is the x-ray of my foot before the surgery and the right is the x-ray of it after! My surgery was on 2/23/26. I had lapiplasty, with a donor bone graft. So far recovery is going better than I anticipated. The nerve block wore off the day after surgery and the pain was horrible. Before it wore off, I felt no pain at all. I am taking journavx, hydrocodone/acetaminophen for pain. I started the journavx the day of my surgery, but held off on the hydrocodone/acetaminophen until the pain started. Once the pain started, the hydrocodone was able to significantly reduce it. My doctor gave me the go ahead to take two of the hydrocodone at first, since the one I took wasn't helping. He also said I could take it every 4 hours, instead of 6. This was absolutely needed for the first few days, as the pain would start coming back badly after 4 hours. I have began weaning off of the hydrocodone, while continuing the journavx and acetaminophen. I am getting to where the pain isn't returning as quickly/badly. It has been 9 hours since I last took hydrocodone, and it still isn't hurting me! I had the splint removed today, and was given a boot to wear! So now I'm able to wear my foot icepack, which feels soooo good! I'm also working on moving my ankle and toes up and down. I'm continuing to elevate my foot as much as possible, and using a knee scooter to get around. I also have knee pads that I wear that help when I have to get down on the ground with my son. Hoping for fast healing and progress!

body: Apparently, I have a few things going on here: 1. Bunion - had it for years but became more pronounced this year where you can see it poking out a little in my Hoka's. 2. Hallux Limitus- stiff big toe & starting to lean inward. 3. Lateral deviation of the sesamoid bone positioning. X-ray showed the one was on the right of my big toe metatarsal instead of underneath it where it should be. I believe this is what's causing me the most pain walking. I've been having trouble walking correctly where I can't push off the ball of my foot and put pressure on my toes and noticing my walking stance is now leaning towards the outside of my right foot. Noticing pain in my hips and lower back too, so I think this could be a domino effect. I've been limited to only wearing my Hoka's even to work bc other shoes just hurt. Upon the doctor telling me I could get a Cortizone shot there's really nothing else I can do because I have orthotics already, I brought up surgery (she did not) & she said she can fix this with surgery. My gut is telling me to go for it now that my deductible has been met and get it over with, but not sure if I need to research this doctor more and even how to do that. I like her but she's no frills, gets you in & out, and her last Google reviews are from 2017 and prior. Any advice is welcome. Should I bother getting a second opinion?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

treatments_mentioned: X-ray, cortisone, ice therapy, orthotics, surgery

body: PF diagnosed in 2015. Custom orthotics + PT off/on for a year. 2016 PF partial rupture in right foot. Boot for 3 months. 2017 - 8k spent in PT, shockwave, acupuncture. No improvements. 2018 PF partial tare in the left foot. Boot for 2 months until I could tolerate the pain without. Half a dozen orthotics over the years. Various trials and doses of cortisone injections every 3 months from 2019-2024.

comments: Yes a good night splint will give you relief from morning pain.
If you have a tear, worst thing you could do.
Castor oil massage and cotton sock on for night, every night

No made my PF worse. Castor oil and magnesium oil together with a sock on. Also deep tissue massage on calves or else use a massage gun as tight calf muscles cause PF. My PF didn't get better until I did this. Pain now gone by 90%.
Strengthen your glutes and hips and legs

Yes! But I cannot wear it all night. I put it on about 3 hours before my alarm goes off. Then I'm actually able to get out of bed. I walk around in it for just a bit. After taking it off, I put on this little strap on heel cushion to walk around in the house.
Yes! But this one the only one I can stand to sleep in. Barely notice it

body: So I've been on oral terbinafine for a bit over a month (no side effects, I was nervous). My big toenails are just slightly yellow at the tips in the corners but I've had the infection for a long time, I just use lots of topicals to keep it at bay. So they aren't a very extreme case. I want to try for a last baby once I'm done with the three month course of medicine, which means I really need to make sure the medicine works.

After dealing with fungus for nearly two decades and trying almost every remedy, I had a podiatrist finally talk me into taking oral medication - Terbinafine. The left is from April 8th and the right is from today (September 25th)! I didn't have any side effects but I also don't drink coffee or alcohol. We checked my liver in the beginning, then halfway which showed elevated numbers. She had me stop for a month, then retest my liver, which was normal again, and I finished.

body: I keep reading having an unhealthy diet gives you toenail fungus! As a former professional footballer for 22 years I have had the best dietician, personal trainers fitness programmes I've maintained very good fitness all my life and a healthy balanced diet. I have never had any toenail issues until 15 years ago after my career. I shared changing rooms with many other team mates including showers etc.

Having this surgery is 100% an exhausting recovering and then all of the sudden one day it gets better. I'm 6 months out and zero pain, totally happy with the results and I could care less that I have a scar. It's way better than having my bunion. Also, I deliver mail - im on my feet for long hours every day and I still have zero pain. BUT one thing I do want to mention to this group is I have Federal Blue Cross Blue Shield. I usually have $300-500 copays for surgeries (like when I had my hysterectomy). For this surgery I have a $5000 copay!!!! Why? Because for implants I have a 30% deductible. I never knew, never had an implant before - my point is, some hospitals will let u know your costs up front. Mine did not. I just assumed it would be my typical copay. I definitely was not expecting a titanium bolt to cost my insurance $40,000 and my copay to be $5,000. It would have been more but I met my out of pocket max for the year. So, I healed great from my foot surgery and extremely happy with everything except for my huge bill.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Hi, my daughter (almost 15) had lapiplasty on Friday. One of the best and most experienced podiatrists in New England. He said everything went well, no issues. But When Block wore off on Saturday the pain got worse and worse until it was Unbearable. Pain meds were completely ineffective. We went to ER and were admitted to pediatrics. Morphine drip, loosening of bandaging and she's now back to Tylenol, advil & oxy rotation and her pain is down from 10 to 2. We were under the impression that this was the least invasive & painful, and most effective long term solution but now I'm having regrets and worried about all of the horror stories I'm reading about. Seems like 90% of the people who have reported their experiences have regrets, long and painful recovery full of setbacks. She's supposed to go back to school in a boot on Dec 2 but from what I've read, that seems far fetched. Did we make a mistake? I feel like we should have left well enough alone. UPDATE: Monday 11/24 She's feeling much better after 24 hours in the hospital which included a morphine drip and cuts in the bandage to loosen certain areas that were too tight. That got pain under control. We met with the surgeon this morning and he did a bandage re-wrap with light pressure and said everything looked as it should. Her pain now is minimal, she hasn't even mentioned it all day & she took her first shower since Thursday. We're much more hopeful and optimistic than we were when I posted this.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

My big toe sticks up like a thumb, still very swollen and it feels like I can feel the screws. I was told to wait for the swelling to go down. Struggle to sleep as it's very uncomfortable and annoying. Maybe it's just the swelling. It's on the ball of the foot under the big toe. Anyone else feel this sensation?

Thanks for the add! Here are pics of my progress so far. Pics of hardware before and after and 2 week and 4 week progress. How long do you feel the stiffness inside your foot? My dr said to expect swelling for up to 6 months.

Okay I'm on here a lot but I've never had this type of surgery I am having issues with the swelling. It has gone down because I can tell you exactly where my screws are because it hurts a bit when something touches it and it's still swollen there. Should I be worried? It isn't horrid pain feels like a pinch then after awhile it goes away. Its a little shiny but it's like I am starting to get feeling back in my foot and it freaks me out.

Finally scheduled surgery for my r-foot at my appt this morning. It's scheduled for June 3rd! It'll be with the same podiatrist from last year! Procedure will be Scarf Bunionectomy w/Reese OR Akin Osteotomy, nerve block injection and w/MAC anesthesia. Looking forward to a successful procedure, zero complications, an uneventful yet positive recovery journey, pt and no longer suffering from bunion pain.

body: Hi everyone. Former athlete here. At the age of 20, I started experiencing pain in my right big toe, and around the same time I developed chronic muscle tightness in my calves. Over the years, this tightness gradually spread to my thighs, glutes, and lower back. The pain in my big toe and the muscle symptoms occur only during walking or running, not at rest. After an MRI evaluation, it was found that I have very advanced cartilage degeneration (hallux rigidus) in my right big toe joint, while my left foot shows no structural issues at all. After many years of conservative treatments, I'm now considering first MTP joint fusion surgery, together with proper gait rehabilitation.

body: Hi ....I have ocd contamination and toenail fungus. It's so hard to have the conditions together. I tried to console my mind and looked online and found out about dermophytes. The fungi that can can cause toenail fungus. It has made me maybe even feel worse. These dermophytes if you have toenail fungus can be in your home...maybe in dust too. You can look online. I'm a senior and I isolate myself. Any thoughts? Its hard enough dealing with fungus but the ocd makes it unbearable.

I have fought (and learned to live with) toenail fungus for 15 years. If it's easy enough to contract it at the gym or the pool, why haven't my husband or kids caught it? Is this the case with any of you?

I've had severe toenail fungus for 18 years! I've tried everything. Finally this year, i feel like I'm seeing huge improvement. - castor oil, tea tree, tinactin cream and Jublia. Wow. Two weeks ago, rashes popped up on my groin, armpits and on my head. Great... athletes foot has spread I assume. I made a doc appt which I couldn't get in for three weeks. So in the mean time, I thought I'd just adapt my diet- 100% cut sugar, refined foods, and alcohol. I'm also taking a probiotic and a biotin supplement. The results have been incredible.

So.... podiatrist appt today. Not ingrown. He said it was curved tho and cut it down. Not so scarey. Using topical I got my md to order from compound pharmacy containing antifungals and dmso to penetrate the nail. When he cut it, most of the fungus is gone. The other toe still has some fungus tho. I'm still using anti fungal and soaking. So hoping maybe I got it early enough...who knows. He said he thought my feet looked good tho so here's hoping.

I cut my big toenails all the way down, as far as I could get them. You can see the fungus on the skin underneath. White clumps. Scraped them off. Not so scarey. Using topical I got my md to order from compound pharmacy containing antifungals and dmso to penetrate the nail. I am applying antifungal cream to the skin and washing in a zinc wash each day. Hoping for some results.

I never experienced these recurrent fungal infections before. Got a fungal infection on my big toe on the right leg last year and I didn't even know that was a fungal infection. Left it like that for 2 months and it got worse. I ended up removing that toe nail and it's still growing. It's been 7 months since the removal and 70 percent of the nail is grown. I have been told to use ketocanazole ointment everyday until the nail is fully grown. I am dealing with other healing issues as well.

Quite hard to show here but my nails are relatively smooth on top but thick underneath. The thick stuff underneath isn't fileable with an electric nail file or any other file. Its almost like thickened/hard skin and not a hard/brittle texture so I am unable to file it. Its quite painful to prod and if I get the file on it, it'll bleed. Any recommendations on getting rid of this?

Sharing what I think is some success. I've been dealing with this gross nail for over 10 years. Four rounds of terbinafine orally, three rounds of laser, and countless home remedies in between with no success. I had been seeing ads for a magic cure in a paintbrush form. Because I'm on the frugal side, I decided to find the active ingredient and try that. Active ingredient was undecylenic acid.

Hey group members any suggesting on what I can do. My 3rd toenail on left foot is the worst. It's so thick and gunk is underneath the nail. My right big toe is completely hard and it actually looks like it's spreading to all of my other toes.

I've just been to a specialist that deals with foot issues and he has prescribed doxycycline and piroxicam tablets to me. To help with inflammation as he reckons you need to support the tendons to help with plantar fasciitis. I recently had mri, so he has seen my results. Has anyone been given these to try before?

body: I had post op #3 yesterday. To say I'm so disappointed with my healing is an understatement. Left foot was done in May 2025, it feels great. No pain, range of motion in my big toe is not great but it doesn't hurt. My right foot was done December 2025, minimally invasive bunion surgery, a couple screws and realigned the bones. I'm 4 months post op. I have zero bone growth. Pain, swelling, hurts to touch my foot, still limping and on pain medication, back in the boot and no driving. Has anyone else gone through this? Where the bone doesn't grow? I'm supposed to get a bone stimulator in the next 1-6 weeks. My dr said if by 9 months there is still nonunion of the bone then I have to have another surgery to remove the hardware and put a plate in, obviously I do not want that. I'd really like to hear from others that have gone through something similar. I'm feeling so discouraged right now.

body: The different shockwave therapies. People get these confused. The first 2 are rarely effective. The last one ESWT is extremely effective but only a few doctors in the US own the machine. ESWT uses the same machine that is used to bust up kidney stones. It targets calcified tissue in the foot for people with advanced plantar fasciitis. The main differences: One time procedure instead of a bunch of sessions. Must be fully numbed with injections. Works for most everyone who has calcification.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

Had a bunionectomy with osteotomy of first metatarsal on 1/26. Had my post op today 1/28 doc said everything is looking good. Hopefully can start walking on it next week. On Percocet for pain but only have 20 pills left and not sure what I am gonna be taking after this.
Praying I have MIS bunion, hammertoe surgery first week of June. I have no pain right now. I'm a nervous wreck already about how my pain will be after the surgery.

body: Hello. My name is Michael. I have been a mechanic since 1994. I wear work boots everyday. I developed toe nail fungus many years ago. I've tried everything to rid myself of the ugly toe. I've tried Vicks. Nothing. I've tried pills which I felt did more harm than good. I've recently gone Holistic. I tried foot soaks with ACV and thought my whole foot does not have issues. However, ACV was Natural. I wanted to concentrate on just the affected toenails.
products_mentioned: Vicks, Vicks VapoRub, apple cider vinegar
treatments_mentioned: Vicks, Vicks treatment, apple cider vinegar, soaking, vinegar soak

body: I'm not sure what's more annoying... when someone says Try Vicks or when someone says I bathe my feet 3 times a day and use an electric nail file each week I also buy anti fungal washing powder and wash all my socks and towels separately. Yes it may work if your nail fungus is mild to moderate but some of us have severe toenail fungus that needs much stronger treatment.

For those of you using something like Vicks or Emuaid which are thick, oily and not totally absorbed... how do you ensure the product isn't just rubbed off or create a big mess? I have athlete's foot and multiple nails with fungus, so I am currently using Emuaid on basically my WHOLE foot just started a week ago.
Has anyone tried Rowe Casa feet and nail salve? I've been trying a combination of filing, tea tree and Vicks for the past year but it doesn't get rid of it completely.
products_mentioned: Vicks, Vicks VapoRub, tea tree oil
treatments_mentioned: Vicks, Vicks treatment, tea tree oil

body: Most people that post here are clearly in desperate need for help and have severe toenail fungus. They have clearly tried everything and are looking for a miracle cure, the truth is there isn't one otherwise we would all be using the same product. It's also an insult when someone says try Vicks!! The most accessible easiest purchase product to get hold of over the counter or online. Yes, don't you think we've tried that already.
comments: I don't agree. I have been trying to find a cure for about 8 years. I have had my big toe nail removed, tried all sorts of treatments from the pharmacy, most to no avail. A year ago I decided to commit to a programme of foot health home treatments. This involves soaking my feet in warm water with a few drops of tea tree for 10 mins.

I've got bad toe nail fungus on 6 of my toes, 3 on each foot, and have had it for a good 15 years. I've been using Vicks on my toes nails since June last year and I have seen a huge improvement. I apply it every day after my shower and will continue to do it for ever if I need to.
I was in exactly same boat! I did find a miracle cure - look up phrena - it actually works. Have used for 2 months and have two-thirds healthy nail regrowth.
If you haven't tried boric acid please do. You will be pleased with the results. The acidity prevents growth. It will be the best 10 dollars you ever spent towards this problem. Natures cure.
I made the decision, I am going to go ahead and shave my nails down as thin as possible and have them remove them. We will see! Even permanent removal is better than this at this point.
Betadine/iodine. File your nails down so they are porous enough for the solution to get to the nail bed. Keep them clipped short - do this for 6 months or more so the nail can grow out.
I agree, so frustrating. Seem someone would be able to come up with a cure.

I did the course of meds and it completely disappeared and grew out. That's the only thing that worked. Then few months later back again same place. Had it over 5 years. Tried everything apart from amputation.

I had the infection for 20+ years and it was getting worse. I took the oral tablets. I also regularly visited a foot dr and used the nail varnish. I had success and now have one infected small toe which I'm treating with varnish. I removed all sugar, fasted 16/8 and ate mostly real food. Never reuse socks unless washed.
products_mentioned: Vicks, betadine, Vicks VapoRub, vicks, tea tree oil

body: Week 9 PO. I just started walking without the help of a crutch last Wednesday... still very challenging with a very swollen foot. I can only fit it in 1 shoe IF I'm wearing a compression sock lol I'll take it but I have felt very bloated all over my body.. both feet, ankles, legs, stomach, arms and hands. Has anyone else dealt with this all over edema? I'm guilty of not drinking enough water every single day, but for the most part I am. I thought with more movement it would get better.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Hi everyone, I hope you are all doing well. I wanted to give an update after my 2 weeks post op appointment. I had hardware removal, triplane osteotomy of the hindfoot, 1st metatarsal wedge osteotomy, heel slide osteotomy, and a bone marrow aspiration from the proximal tibia. They used a dynanail mini and a compression screw to give the best chance of healing at the hindfoot with constant compression. My surgeon and surgeon's fellow feel that everything is looking good so far.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group
comments: Good to hear from you so glad you are doing well, let's hope this is it xxx sending love and wishing speedy recovery

body: Positive story so far. Had lapiplasty in January of this year on the right foot. Not gonna lie it was living hell the first few days. But my bones fused in record time (surgeon's words) and its been pretty seamless since. Still some swelling and tenderness but every day is a little better. Had MIS surgery today on my left foot. So far its been painless! He numbed the foot but no nerve block. He doesn't like doing them. Thinks it causes nerve damage. About yeeted myself out of the surgery center before surgery. The nurse informed me that it was going to be an hour and 45 minutes and I was getting a general anesthesia. Surgeon initially told me 30 minutes and under twilight. Will be partial weight bearing tomorrow as tolerated. So far I'm glad I did both feet and hope to be walking without bunion pain soon! It's to see what does better...just screws or a plate and screws.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Recovery update after my June 11, 2025 bunion repair (Lapiplasty with correction of the bunion with Calcaneal Bone Graft and lesser toe corrections): Non-weight bearing for 4 weeks to walking in boot for 4 weeks. Then transitioned to a tennis shoe for 12 weeks of physical therapy and returned to work in October on modified duty. After returning, I started having pain under the outside of my ankle toward pinkie toe. I saw my surgeon again.

body: I have always liked Teva sandals, I messaged them and got this prompt reply. This is for UK customers. body: My before and after. Surgery on 12/18. Just had my 6 weeks. Doc told me everything looks great and I'm healing well.

Does anyone have any guidelines on the weight-bearing timelines post op? I believe I've seen a lot of 0% weight bearing the first 2 weeks post op, but then what? I'm at 6-weeks post op and the surgical site and even my hammertoe correction seems to be recovering well, but every few days I get the most wicked sprained ankle pain to the point my ankles are purple. And I know it's because I'm compensating. I feel if I can start putting 50% weight on my entire foot it would help out my ankle a lot. When does bone fully heal anyway?

I need to hear some MIS SUCCESS stories! Let me hear from the people who have had no issues, who have healed and recovered as they were told they would. I feel like the horror stories take center stage. I am 10 days PO, 50% weight-bearing, and see my surgeon tomorrow for my 1st PO visit/suture removal. Thus far, I have had no pain and have only needed ibuprofen with the exception of day 5 after a minor stumble I took an actual pain pill.

body: What has truly worked for successfully recovering from toenail fungus?

Has anyone tried a product called Bare Feet? It's a capsule and advertised and talked about by a Pharmacist named Lisa. Claims it cures the Fungus, not just camouflages the ugly nail. It is between 40 and 60 dollars a bottle. They claim success in weeks but then offers a years supply which sounds contradictory to me. We'd like to hear of your experience.

Hi I have OCD contamination and toenail fungus. It's so hard to have the conditions together. I tried to console my mind and looked online and found out about dermatophytes. The fungi that can cause toenail fungus. It has made me maybe even feel worse. These dermatophytes if you have toenail fungus can be in your home maybe in dust too. I'm a senior and I isolate myself. Any thoughts?

Anyone ever say forget it, it's not going anywhere anyway and just put polish on or a fake nail just so you can wear flip flops or sandals? I know they say it makes it worse, but this feels like a life sentence. I need my toes out, and that's not cute.

I have fought and learned to live with toenail fungus for 15 years. If it's easy enough to contract it at the gym or the pool, why haven't my husband or kids caught it? Is this the case with any of you?

Dear lovely friends, please can I ask your advice? I am scheduled to have a meeting with a new surgeon who is an expert in the field. He wants to do revision surgery. The first surgery I had on the NHS went wrong because the surgeon did not correct the hammer toes and also left a big toe floating in the air and pressing pretty hard against the second toe. I am therefore now in more pain than I was before the first surgery.

Does anyone have a good website to buy the Correct Toes Stable Toes? The official website is charging crazy postage and Yoga Matters is out of stock! I'm desperate to get something else as my silicone ones have started to hurt my poor second toe. Yes, I'm already wearing Altra and Topo, sized up in mens and looking like a hobbit.

body: Positive story so far! Had Lapiplasty in January of this year on the right foot. Not gonna lie it was living hell the first few days. But my bones fused in record time (surgeon's words) and its been pretty seamless since. Still some swelling and tenderness but every day is a little better. Had MIS surgery today on my left foot. So far its been painless! He numbed the foot but no nerve block. He doesn't like doing them, thinks it causes nerve damage. About yeeted myself out of the surgery center before surgery. The nurse informed me that it was going to be an hour and 45 minutes (right foot was 2 hrs) and I was getting a general anesthesia. Surgeon initially told me 30 minutes and under twilight. He clarified that it was going to be about 45 minutes and still MIS. In my 10 past surgeries for various things I've never had twilight. Scared me to think I could wake up hearing them drilling into my bone! I'm in a randomized study for a year to see what does better - just screws or a plate and screws. They basically flipped a coin and picked an envelope. Will be partial weight bearing tomorrow as tolerated. So far I'm glad I did both feet and hope to be walking without bunion pain soon!
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group
conditions_mentioned: nerve damage, swelling, edema, neuropathy, bunion

body: I have OCD contamination and toenail fungus. It's so hard to have the conditions together. I tried to console my mind and looked online and found out about dermatophytes. The fungi that can cause toenail fungus. It has made me maybe even feel worse. These dermatophytes if you have toenail fungus can be in your home, maybe in dust too. I'm a senior and I isolate myself. Any thoughts? It's hard enough dealing with toenail fungus without the OCD making it worse.

So good and bad news from the podiatrist today. My left toe next to my pinky feels like someone is squeezing it. Sometimes really painful, sometimes not. I got an ingrown toenail! And of course that toe has fungus on it. Referral to podiatrist, just have to wait a couple weeks. My PCP gave me antibiotics and told me to soak it too. At least it is a fairly easy fix!

Just a note for those considering oral meds for toenail fungus - I didn't have a single side effect taking terbinafine for 3 months and my liver wasn't affected at all, it stayed normal. I don't drink alcohol or coffee and never have done drugs so my liver is likely better than most. I did have to take pain meds on my period which I was worried about but still no side effects. My nail fungus was mild but had lasted over 7 years. It was just the big toenail that was affected.

My doctor put me on Lamisil oral and it almost put me into liver failure. So I'm back to square one with my toenail fungus. Has been there for a couple months now. Looking for alternative treatments that won't risk my liver health.
Night splints... please share pros and cons and if I should be wearing them for plantar fasciitis. Thanks so much.

I also wear Brooks Women's Glycerin GTS 21 Supportive Running Shoe. I highly recommend them.

body: 1.) Get Kerasal and use as directed. 2) Make a mixture of hydrogen peroxide and Listerine. Use this at least 2x daily. More often if possible. 3) Use a manicure tool to open up the edges of the fungal infested nails, and scoop out the white gunk that accumulates.

It gives me a giggle to know I have fungus at 25. I've had it for 2 years now. With nothing helping or curing it. I just recently used some kerasal to make my nail mushy to get rid of the icky under the nail plate. I have TRIED multiple different things. That one medication everyone raves about from the doctors. Nail polish type things. Oils. Creams. The lot. I think I might schedule an appointment with the podiatrist again. Make them remove my putrid toenail.

So I've been filing it and pieces are coming off. Last summer the whole nail itself came off but unfortunately grew back same thing. I'm too afraid of the removal. I have proclearz, kerasal, clarus but I don't think it will matter if it's this bad unfortunately.

body: Hi everyone! I'm not cured but I have been able to make a number of adjustments to live with less pain day-to-day, and I have even started running again. First, I started using Voltaren gel, a number of months back. It has really helped with my tendonitis and neuroma pain. I did the loading dose for a few weeks and now I use it 2x per day and as needed. Ice packs! Next, I found hiking sandals from Kuailu. Foot massager with heat. Powerstep inserts and heel cups. For running I use a treadmill on rubber flooring. Built up to about 30 mins/2 miles with intervals.

body: Oofos. Hi has anyone purchased oofos and how have you found them. Thinking of these for the house.

I got some Oofos slides with adjustable velcro on the top for Christmas - asked for them on my gift list because I started getting plantar fasciitis in my feet I think from walking barefoot when in the house so trying to keep good arch support shoes on my feet in the house. I like the Oofos for the arch support but on the right foot the slide is rubbing on my bunion and making it sore - anyone have any ideas on something I could put there?

So podiatrist appt today. Not ingrown. He said it was curved tho and cut it down. Not throbbing now, so we will see. When he cut it, most of the fungus is gone. The other toe still has some fungus tho. I'm still using anti fungal and soaking. So hoping maybe I got it early enough. He said he thought my feet looked good tho so here's hoping.

Hi has anyone had all their toenails removed with bed before? I would like some photos once all healed. I am looking to get this done to mine as I have tried everything including the tablets 3 times and come back worse each time.

An old friend tells me that tea tree oil cleared up his toenail fungus. I looked on amazon.com and there are dozens of brands to choose from. Can someone recommend one for me to try. I can't tolerate fragrances and perfumes so I'd prefer one with no additives.

Has anyone experienced stabbing nerve pain going into their big toenail? This started happening to me a few days ago and it's torture. The only things that help are putting Biofreeze on it and taking Percocet. I have been filing my nail down really thin and putting Nonyx nail gel on it and antifungal cream for 2 years. Maybe doing these things for so long irritated a nerve in my toe? I went to urgent care today and they gave me Gabapentin.

Hello, new here. Ten years, three podiatrists and one foot nurse later, here I am. I was told all this time I had thickened toenails due to repeated trauma, ill fitting shoes etc. Previous tests negative until the most recent one. I've tried everything over the years, listerine, epsom salts, all the topicals, ACV, Urea. Doctor unwilling to prescribe oral, says liver risk outweighs benefits. Recently started Toe Fx (Canada but I think in US soon). After significantly grinding the nails down and applying it daily I am seeing improvement.
treatments_mentioned: apple cider vinegar, epsom salt, vinegar soak

body: Thank you for adding me. Went to a podiatrist today and he wanted to remove the nail, which now after joining this group I am glad I didn't. I asked him what the redness was on my skin and he was noncommittal. My nurse practitioner first thought it was cellulitis, so I did a round of doxycycline which of course was no help. Does toenail fungus make your toe painful and swollen? I am going to try the compounded topical lamisil, because I cannot do oral meds. Has anyone had success with that?

Does it ever get better? Every time I make some progress then it comes back 10x worse. I'm in so much pain but I'm terrified to take the pills prescribed. I'm so sensitive to medication I know it's going to really mess me up. I just wish I could heal this naturally but I'm losing hope that will ever happen. Right now it's the worst it's ever been and it's spread all the way back into my cuticle even and my entire toe just throbs.

Summer Shoes/Sandals with Toe Nail Fungus - The warmer weather is approaching. Those of you who've had issues last summer, what type of shoes/sandals did you wear in the summer? I've always worn open toe sandals in the past and nail polished pedicures. I would like to let my toes breathe instead of wearing closed toe shoes/sneakers but without nail polish, I'm embarrassed to wear sandals this summer.

My friend Sarita is on a mission to end foot pain. I've watched my friend Sarita struggle for over 11 years with a painful foot deformity. I saw her try every bulky, uncomfortable brace out there, and I saw how much it hurt her just to go for a walk. Instead of staying stuck, she spent the last few years inventing her own solution: toe spacer socks that actually feel good and work with normal shoes.

For about a year I have had increasing pain on lateral side of one foot and below 4th and 5th metatarsal joint. I know the fat pads are practically non existent and have relied on memory foam insoles to provide some degree of comfort. Now I discover my foot is overly supinated and I use heel wedges to compensated with some degree of success. I really don't want to undergo surgery as I am an anesthetic risk from bronchiectasis.

I am looking for insole suggestions. Please help me. I have Morton's toe and flat feet which means my second and third toe joint are what strikes first and no arch to support and I've had chronic stress fractures because of it. I don't have insurance right now and I'm trying to find a good insert I can put in my shoes so I can exercise without causing too much pain.

Hi everyone, just joined the group as I'm just looking for some support from people who get it. I feel like family and friends just don't, which is understandable. I basically had a full foot reconstruction. They realigned my heel, rebuilt my arch, fused part of my midfoot, corrected my big toe, straightened my 4th toe, and put in several screws to hold everything in place. It was quite a big op to fix my collapsed foot and stop it getting worse. I got run over by a bus as a child which caused the damage.

Ordered new custom orthotics for my horrifically flat feet. I've been waiting 7 weeks. They took moulds of my feet and then they were sent from uk to Australia where they are made. Hoping they make the difference for ankle tendonitis. I've been off past 2 months with this - physio says muscles are very weak. The exercises I was given hurt but I'm doing them - dread going back to work.
Recommended new custom orthotics which fit and physio for peroneal tendonitis due to flat feet. Had physio few years back on other foot which found tough - anyone any tips?

Has anyone had nerve conduction test? First of all I've had foot pain for years dealing with plantar fasciitis. I get flare ups from time to time from overuse but it's manageable. In November I broke my fifth metatarsal. Spent 8 weeks in a boot. Just now getting around well. Now I get this weird tingling sensation in my arch. Dr recommended a nerve conduction test. It's expensive and was hoping to get some insight on how it works. Anyone familiar with?

body: People with diagnosed diabetic neuropathy in their feet do your feet go through different stages of temp, tingling vs sharp pain, different areas of constant pain vs shooting pain or do these symptoms happen all the time 24/7?

Hi I have had foot pain since February and although I go to the hospital next week I'm still unsure. The pain is different each day and the severity. body: Healing & recovery must-haves! I just finished my 3rd surgery on my feet, this time to remove hardware from one, and bone spurs and a neuroma removed from the other. Both had previous lapidus bunion removals with fusion, plus one had 2 toes shortened & the other had 2 hammertoes repaired. These 3 items have kept me much more pain free through all my surgeries, and have helped scarring immensely. For nerve pain, I take Alpha Lipoic Acid and Tumeric Curcumin daily. For scarring & bruising I start using Castor Oil right after stitches come out. They work wonders for me! If you are struggling, just know that it does get better and you will get your life back. Hang in there! Much love to everyone

body: 6 weeks post-op for 1st toe MTP fusion, 2nd toe hammertoe correction, neuroma removal from between second and third toe, and capsule release of 3rd toe. I'm still wearing a surgical shoe and have been allowed to heel walk since surgery. I hope to be allowed to wear shoes, walk normally and ride a stationary bike after my x-ray on Wednesday. I've also been driving since going off the percoset after the first week post-op. I truly believe that the skill of my surgeon has made for a very manageable surgery. If you're in the Phoenix area and need a surgeon, Stanley Graves, M.D. is who I recommend.

body: An update to my last post. I've been in my new Altra FWD Vias for 4 days now (after 3 days in Crocs while I waited), and for the first time in my life I've worn shoes that give my toes space to spread as I walk. That toe spread is what's healing me. For years, my toes were smushed together in traditional shoes, so my arches never actually worked. Instead, my heels and calves carried the load, which is why my PF flared.

body: Bowen therapy! I'd never heard of it till I met someone who'd had it, she showed me her feet, both had had bunions, one had surgery about 10 years ago, the other Bowen therapy and I couldn't tell the difference. I'm 2 sessions in and I can see a noticeable straightening of the toes, I'm so happy. It's a holistic straightening of the skeleton and releasing of the muscles which helps alignment, posture etc. I really think it's worth trying before surgery.

body: My toes looked bad three weeks ago. I started soaking in apple cider vinegar and mouthwash 50-50 then after soaking very carefully cutting back the bad part of the nail with a flat razor blade. I've added colloidal silver with dropper rubbing it in waiting a bit then putting a light coating of antifungal cream then Vicks vapor rub and then I put socks on. I do it in the morning and night and also during the day once in a while. I also use a Dremel with an abrasive stone and grind it down once week. That seems to really help get the medicine in. The soaking will change the ph to a level that fungi does not like it will also kill any fungi you have on your foot, mine were dry and cracked and now they are much better. I also put anti fungal cream over all of my foot as well. It does not completely get rid of the fungi because when I stop being diligent it roars right back, usually over winter.
treatments_mentioned: dremel, apple cider vinegar, anti-fungal medication, soaking, Vicks treatment, Vicks, vinegar, antifungal, vinegar soak
products_mentioned: dremel, mouthwash, colloidal silver, apple cider vinegar, Vicks VapoRub, Vicks, vicks

body: For those of you that had your right foot operated on, when were you able to drive after surgery?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group
comments: I waited the 6 weeks to drive locally. Drove long distance for the first time this Thursday and I'm at 3 months post op.
I had a cast for 8 weeks then a boot for two weeks. So, I drove after 10 weeks.
I've yet to have surgery but at my 1st consultation, I was told it would be 4 months before I could drive.
Legally, you're not allowed to drive if you have a boot on your foot
After my first bunion surgery, my doc gave me a driving shoe so I was able to drive at two weeks. I've recently had fusion surgery and I'm 3 weeks out and can't drive yet.
I was cleared to initially drive short distances, 10-15 mins from our house, once I was able to get my foot into a sneaker which was at 3 months post-op.
I waited 7 weeks. Need to be able to do an emergency stop safely or your insurance is invalid.
When I felt safe that I could perform an emergency stop without damaging my feet, bilateral 6th Jan drove short distances 18th Feb.
8 weeks had both feet done
I was told 6 weeks - see my surgeon this Thursday for the 6 week check so praying he says I'm good to go! Ready to get my life back now.
treatments_mentioned: boot/walking boot, cast, cast immobilization, surgery

I don't have insurance to go to a podiatrist, but I'd like your opinion. Also, would it be safe to put an acrylic toe nail over it so I can wear sandals?

Is it painful having the big toe nail removed? I'm scared. Edited to say I have a fungal big toe I banged it and it lifted and bled. I'm waiting to see the podiatrist and they said they'd have to remove it so I don't know if it's permanent or not yet. I'm just scared to have the bandage taken off and anything done as it's so sensitive.
comments: I had both of my big toenails removed at the exact same time and I will tell you it sucked the first few days. I was limping bad. But it is totally worth it and my fungus did not grow back with the nails fortunately.
Take some ibuprofen with you. When the numbness wore off it was bad until ibuprofen kicked in
Had mine done in January... didn't feel it while they were doing it but it was very sore in the days to follow. So far so good with it healing. Happy got it done.
First night is the worst get through that and you'll be fine.
Don't be scared, I've been through it and can tell you it's worth it in the end, getting mine removed early really helped and now I'm all good
Can I ask if it grows back or is it permanent?
A podiatrist will give you a local anaesthetic. It will be painless. Bit sore after local wears off. Take NSAIDs and Paracetamol if you're allowed. Temporary best if it's appropriate.
Are you doing temporarily or permanent? I had mine done permanent and it sucks.

I had one removed in 2022 it was very painful but Dr admitted it shouldn't have hurt that much and there's a possibility he didn't numb it enough as I felt everything during removal. It's grown back worse for me too.

Mine was surprisingly easy. Part of the removal did hurt, a lot. I didn't want to wait for another anesthetic injection to take effect so I just gritted my teeth for a few more seconds. After I was bandaged up, the recovery was fine.

body: Is anyone else 8 weeks post op and is not able to walk yet and has stiffness in toes and swelling?

comments: I was still in a boot at 8 weeks. Started to walk in a shoe at 10 weeks but had a bad limp and it was painful. Started PT and I've had 6 sessions. It's made a huge difference. No limp, I'm walking on the treadmill with no pain. My doctor told me that I will notice the biggest improvement between 3-6 months. This surgery will definitely test your patience. I'm at 16 weeks and finally feeling normal.
Almost 9 weeks post op. I am walking almost normally, but still mild discomfort when applying full pressure on the big toe side. I had lapiplasty. My ankle was and still is slightly weak
Have you went through physical therapy? Definitely going through physical therapy and religiously doing the exercises will help with better mobility.

Yes! I also had ankle surgery too which I'm sure isn't helping. Go tonight to pt eval. Having difficulty moving my toes and they aren't staying in place. Was given a tens unit to try and wake up the nerves too. I'm still in a boot mainly cause I have swelling and can't get into a shoe. But I've been doing some non boot time walking barefoot and need to use crutches to help off set the weight. Dr had me in an ace too to help with the swelling but told me I could stop that. I'm unsure that was the best move. Swelling is happening and now my ankle is sore and stiff.
I am 10 weeks and can walk. Going to PT ..morning stiffness is tough.
I'm 7 weeks. Course I have only been in a boot for 2 weeks with limited amount of time to be up (1 hour total) rest of time elevated. My big toe has some stiffness.
Surgery on Dec 17th. Walking okay. In PT to improve ROM of big toe.

I had some reels coming thru my feed about a month ago on barefoot shoes not like the ones I recall but as you describe with really wide toe box n zero drop that I need to research. And they look good lol. But lately I have started to do toe movement, stretches n exercises specifically for the bones of the foot n toes which has helped along with my calf exercises. Many good vids n reels out there
I started wearing barefoot shoes by Purestep, they along with regular strong calf massages cured my plantar fasciitis
Happy for you\! Zero drops were my answer.
I'm trying Altras. My pain has shifted from inner heel to middle heal..Trying to do Dr Angel stretches.

body: I had lapidus surgery on January 7th and I dont think I am 100% ready to return to work without restrictions or extended medical leave. I returned to work on 19th and 20th last week, putting in about 5 miles of steps each shift, which is about average, and about ready to cry mid shift in the bathroom as my left foot feels so sore, heavy, and refuses to bend. I was limping half way in on the first day and most of the day on Friday.

body: 6 weeks and 4 days PO. Just finished my first day back at work. So glad I work from home\! I'm sore and it's a desk job\! While I couldn't elevate toes above nose, I was able to get it up a little, and ice my foot. It's still swollen and dark almost purple. Doesn't hurt though so I'm all good.

body: I went to a second opinion today\! After 3 surgeries on one foot, I found out it is nerve pain that's been the cause of all my pain\! I was told they will do steroid injections once a month for 3 months, plus to wear scar sheets and scar gel cream. Has anyone had nerve pain? And what have you done to help?
treatments_mentioned: cortisone, cortisone injection, steroid injection, surgery

body: I am happy that I found this group, prompting me to do more research and get a second opinion. I went to a podiatrist a couple months ago and walked out uncertain about the procedure, doctor, and office staff. Today I saw an orthopedic foot surgeon who was very thorough, asked me questions about my lifestyle, health, and goals. The staff from front to back were professional and helpful. I walked out confident and ready to get my right foot done (left foot isn't as bad).

Im only 27 and im producing a very painful tailors bunion. My family doctor is saying not to do surgery but im looking for a second opinion bc in only 27 and its just going to get worse. My question is if i should do surgery or not? Do i do it now and get it out of the way and deal with the arthritis when im older or do the surgery when im older and risk infection and other issues. Idk what to do. A lot of people tell me not to do it and wait, but bc of that fact it doesn't make sense to me to wait? Help....
conditions_mentioned: arthritis, bunion, tailor's bunion, tailors bunion

body: I have a bunion and hammertoe on my right foot. I just saw Podiatrist... No orthopedic surgeons nearby on my plan - I plan on getting a second opinion, but wanted to know if anybody here have any opinions. I have absolutely no pain in my bunion or my hammertoe. The only pain I get is rubbing on shoes. When I wear wide mesh shoes which are totally unattractive I'm OK. Podiatrist thinks I would most benefit from traditional bunion surgery.

I recently saw another doctor for a second opinion and they said the exact same thing. But this doctor showed me my X-ray and examined my arches and seemed much more thorough. I don't want to make the wrong choice with which doctor to use. How do I choose which one to go with. The first or the second?
2 days post surgery. I had the right foot done 10 yrs ago so knew what I was in for\!\! What i didnt expect was not to be given any crutches\!??\! Bloody gd job i have smart crutches

Victoria Rose Baxter asked me to keep you all updated on how my appointment went today. Week 4 after surgery, to the day, I got my stitches out\!\! Woo hoo\!\! I can walk with my boot but I have to say I am scared. It hurt so bad to put weight on it at first. This will take a bit. The best news.......I can shower without wearing the dang boot and actually sit on my shower chair by myself in my shower. I feel like I have died and gone to heaven.
6 Weeks post op\! Goodbye to the crutches & boot\! Hello to weight bearing and shoes\!
treatments_mentioned: boot/walking boot, rest, surgery, weight bearing

body: Hey yall\! I am having the lapiplasty operation on my left foot this June\! I had the Austin procedure done to my right foot in 2022. It was a long hard recovery. But the pain was manageable. Just took a long time for my foot to look normal nevertheless, it changed my life for the better\! And it's time to do the left\! I am in pain all day everyday\! I am wondering if anyone has had the two separate procedures on their feet? Or any insights?
Had surgery on Jan 16. Im very happy with the results. Before and after.

Update- I sent the picture to my foot doctor today and received a phone call back. He says it looks fine. I appreciate everyone's input. And I wish all of us on here a speedy/full recovery\! I had bunion surgery 18 days ago. I was told to take a shower today. I still can't put weight on that foot, it was a little scary. I have a few questions if anyone can give me their take post op. 1- it's the first time I actually looked at my foot.
treatments_mentioned: boot/walking boot, cast, cast immobilization, rest, surgery, taping, weight bearing
comments: Shower stool. I sat and transferred into the shower then turned on water with handheld sprayer. Shower was awesome but yes purple color due to circulation. Its a good thing. Safety always first. It gets better. This is 6 weeks for me.
How red? My foot wasn't red. The big toe pain and numbness is normal.
That is terrific that you are 18 days out and your doctor is saying things are good! I had to see my podiatrist 3 days after surgery to get the dressing changed so I saw my foot.
We have a big walk in shower so I used a walker for support while I showered. One wall has a seat also so I sat for parts.
If you feel that there is a concern - can you take a pic and send it or do a telehealth visit? Trust your gut - better to be safe than sorry.
Everything is normal!!!
I bought a shower stool and sat on that with my cast covered from day 2. I then showered with my knee resting on the stool. I was non weight bearing for 6 weeks.
I'm 13 wks post op. Everything sounds normal. I wore my boot in the shower it helped a lot because it was painful in bare feet. Still is. Just double bag and tape your foot.
I have been showering since the third day after surgery and then about every other day. First, I bought a shower chair. Next, I have a walk in shower which has a hand held shower piece I can use.
Sponge bath

body: When you get a letter from your surgeon saying ...Sorry, never had a complication like yours before. Possible removal of plate and extra screw fixation ahead. Oh and to finish it off. 12 weeks NWB in full plaster.

After over a year my fungal nail is better\! What finally did it was a combination of washing all my clothes with dettol laundry cleaner in. Soaking in Epsom salts, apple cider vinegar and little borax a couple of time a week. Putting H-Nail Fungus and NonyX Nail gel on morning and night and regularly cleaning out under the nail and removing any fungal looking bits
treatments_mentioned: epsom salt, apple cider vinegar, vinegar, vinegar soak, soaking
products_mentioned: Epsom salt, apple cider vinegar, dettol, epsom salt, epsom salts, nonyx

body: Been fighting toenail fungus since 2002. I'm tired of buying drugs and creams that do little to nothing because I fail to take care of the real problem. Anyway, I just took my two foot tubs, filled them with freezing cold water, and put Dish soap and Crest Scope Mouth wash and started scrubbing. My first impressions were a success. The fungus was like a mucus, once I started scrubbing and getting off the problems my feet starts to get better.

Putting this out there in case it helps anyone. I battled for 20yrs with fungus infection with 3 of my toes on just my left foot. Drs had me try everything. Finally this week I was told to see a foot specialist. Little did I know since I had done everything already the only treatment left was to permanently remove them. I did. Best thing ever. They did it right in the office as a chemical removal. The worst part was the infection to numb it. Still healing but just using over the counter pain relief.
Hi my podiatry doctor has recommended hoka trainers as extra wide for my tailors bunion. Has anyone got these ? I'm in UK
products_mentioned: Hoka, topo, hoka, wide toe box shoes
comments: Hoka have a narrow toe box so interesting choice from your podiatrist. I've had a number of pairs but wore men's wide. Have you looked at Topo? I wouldnt wear anything else now. Naturally wide toe box

I recently tried several Hokas. Currently, all of them have a very tall plastic sole that wraps up and over the material. All models pinched the outer edge of my foot due to sitting on top of this edge. Their wides didn't feel much different.

body: Did anyone else get their second and third toes shortened along with a bunion removal? (They are hammer toes. ). I'm so impressed that so many people know the medical terms for their surgery. body: 5 days post surgery. I swear when I had the other foot done 10 yrs ago it wasn't this awkward. They didnt want me using crutches this time but did the st time. I am using smart crutches , they height difference hurts my back! Im finding it very frustrating not being able to.do.everything I usually do!!

One week post bunion surgery update and stitch check
Hi! Tomrw is 1 week post bunion surgery on my right toe. I go this Friday to look at the stitches as it tore open, then may 1st I'll get stiches out. Hoping that'll mean I'll have the boot off about 2 weeks after? Has anyone had the boot off within a month?
Anyone else non weight barring? I'm supposed to be non weight barring for 1 more week!
Hello everyone. 54 almost 55. Had problems all my life.

UK here.
3rd week post double foot op. You all seem to know what was done to your feet. I am waiting until 6 week check to hear what actually happened in the surgery.
Have to say as a person who has had many operations this operation has thrown me. Patience is needed.
My before and after. Surgery on 12/18. Just had my 6 weeks.
6 week check up today all looks good doctor said bone is growing back. Go back in 5 weeks.

I'll be 6 weeks post op tomorrow (Monday) and have my final appt with the surgeon on Wednesday. I have a few questions for this knowledgable group. 
1-How long after surgery until you no longer had swelling and could fit into your pre op shoes? I don't have much swelling but I tried on my shoes last night and it felt very tight and my foot is tender. (I'm still using the surgical shoe but will most likely be told on Wednesday that I can start transitioning to regular shoes.)

I live in New England (close enough to Boston) that I'm looking for a malpractice attorney. I had a second opinion on my feet after 4 foot surgeries to find out the surgeon butchered my feet and I'm basically screwed for life. I'd rather do this annon because I don't need it getting back to the doctor. Thank you in advance.

body:
Injury: Hallux Limitus
Question: Looking for info on morton's extension orthotics
I saw my Sports MD again and he watched me walk again (note, I've been dealing with symptoms for 11 months with MANY misdiagnoses along the way). He feels my issues are related to Hallux Limitus of the big toe. My symptoms are with my first and second toes. My second toe feels very sore at the joint and sometimes swells up.

body:
I have to say this was the best experience! I was reading all the post op posts on here and was scared to death of the recovery after bunion surgery. Posts of so much pain, not being able to bear weight for several weeks, unable to move their big toe without alot of pain and no driving for several months. I cancelled my surgery.
Then I found Foot First Podiatry and Dr Sklar! Holy Moly! The absolute opposite of all the posts! Weight bearing the same day, not on the ball of my foot.
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body:
For over a year now I've been working on going somewhat barefoot.
Seems it's not doing my feet any good. I do wear toe separators (not 100% of the time) and in that time have developed a tailors Bunion (left foot). Little toe still wants to move "in".
2nd and 3rd toe seem "displaced on both feet.
No I'm not wearing arrow shoes.
Have been doing foot exercises...guess it's off to the foot doctor...just hope he can help with prescribing very costly orthotics.

body: Update on the ten little piggies. Soaked today in Epsom Salt for 35 minutes. After drying, Dropper applications of bleach and Tea Tree oil, separately. Obviously they look rough but I can say there is improvement. Also noticed less pressure from the nail bed. Tonight, the treatment will be Dropper applications direct on the toes of bleach, tea tree oil, vicks. Cotton socks for 8 hours afterwards.

body: I guess slow progress is better than no progress. This is about 60 days of brushing bleach on my toenails.
Hey all I've heard that diluting bleach with water and brushing some on your toenail and under your nail can help eliminate fungus. Has anyone tried or heard this before? Thanks

This is after 1 year of treatment with Jublia and I used other things as well. Such as tea tree oil, bleach soaks and applying bleach directly. What helped most was grinding down the nails to almost nothing so the medicine could reach the nail bed. As you can see all toes are almost healed except the big toe I'm still working on and a little toe in the left foot. I also wear sandals everyday.
treatments_mentioned: bleach soak, physical therapy, soaking, tea tree oil

body: I walk 18km then the next day my left foot is severly painful i think its sesamoiditis what should i do? i cant afford to go to the hospital

Is there such a thing as a big toe joint replacement? I have been dealing with chronic pain for years as a result of botched bunionectomy. The botched surgery left me with horrible sesamoiditis, which eventually led to a sesamoidectomy (can't recall at the moment whether it was tibial or medial, but it did nothing for the ball of foot pain). I have very little hope, but always hoping that there will be medical advances for foot surgery.
conditions_mentioned: big toe joint, bunion, foot pain, metatarsalgia, sesamoiditis

body: I have had plantar faciitis & sesamoiditis on and off for years. In the past, used ice, tennis ball, stretches & it calmed down. Now for the past 19 months I have had symptoms of both. I have been in Physical Therapy, seen 3 podiatrists, 4 cortisone shots. PF is better but still ball of foot pain, have tried insoles (insurance will not pay for custom orthotics). Now I have stopped gym & fitness walking. I am at my wit's end.
conditions_mentioned: foot pain, metatarsalgia, plantar fasciitis, sesamoiditis

body: Help!!! Has anyone experienced sesamoiditis, and if so, how have you resolve it?
Can gout cause sesamoiditis? Any tips when dealing with both problems at the same time

Alright update.
Well the doctor is going to put me on gabapentin but he is going to get me a referral to see the neurologist that's in the same building. He will conduct a nerve test on me but also look at my back. Because I did ask him if my back problems could also be related to my feet problems and he said yes that's a possibility. He basically says it could be tarsal tunnel with it only happening with activity.

hey there - newbie here. So 5 yrs ago had knee surgery (microfracture) and after that my gait changed causing all sorts of tendinitis (perennial and Achillies). To add insult to injury, I put on compression sleeves to help, but all it did was cause my tarsal tunnel to get inflamed in both feet which took over a year to calm down from. For years now my feet have been hypersensitive to shoes/sneakers. Any new sneaker/shoe I feel even a seam in.
conditions_mentioned: tarsal tunnel, tarsal tunnel syndrome, tendonitis

body: I've been diagnosed with Plantar Fasciitis & Sinus Tarsi syndrome & chronic postoperative pain... Surgical history tarsal coalition resection and excision of anterior process of the calcaneus fracture fragment. What helped.. Steroid shots which only provided transient benefit for a week.. I may be a candidate for DRG. But all these what ifs.. back to working full time hours. I need to work. But it's so hard to have the drive and continue.

body:
Hi
I removed my big toe nail 2 months ago. I was soaking and doing all things as Dr told me. I am using ketocanozole ointment for the last one month. I still soak the toes in warm water once every 2 days.
I was covering my toe with band aid for most of the time. But Dr told me to keep it open and been doing it only for the last few days.

I have been fighting toenail fungus just on my one big toe for about 5 years now. My husband tells me I just need to have it removed. Which I have made an appointment for June to do so. I'm just wondering if you have had your nail removed and it been successful?
So I have an appointment booked with the podiatrist. I'm getting my nails removed.
I was looking at old posts to see if I saw an answer to this but my toenail fell off today. It's been loose for a while. Does this look normal?

Has anyone had toenail removed and take forever to heal? It's been 1.5 months so far. Infected. Throbbing bad. Antibiotics previously. Cream. Soaks. Mind you same thing happened on other foot 15 years ago. It got so bad my toe turned black and had to go to wound clinic to debris. I was literally 23. I'm nervous same thing gonna happen.
I do have Raynauds which causes circulation issues I think is factor. I'm not diabetic.

Need to start taking terbinafine, need some extra encouragement, please tell me how you felt and please tell me it's not gonna disrupt my life (I have medical anything / health anxiety), not sure why I'm so scared to take it, got foot fungus from pedi's about 15 years ago, empty nested now & want to focus on getting my toenail right and need to do this, please tell me it's gonna be ok taking these and I'm not gonna die lol

Hi all they are still saying i have PF at the the doctors, I've had it for 6 months now, I'm seeing a podiatrist for the first time on December 5th, I'm just wondering if anyone has ever had a stress fracture instead of PF as I'm worried because the pain is so unbearable i have osteopen & i fractured the bottom of my foot last year without knowing about it, after walking around in pain for months, i had an x-ray & it showed the fracture.

body: So dealing with PF for over 18 months, a new podiatrist finally took some action, got a depo-medrol shot and it took my pain down to a 1-2 from a constant 5-6 with a 7-8 in the morning. It's been a week since the shot and foot is feeling better each day. I combined it with new Sof Sol PF insoles as the podiatrist recommended getting a heel cup. And restarted using the night splint. Feeling like my old self again and going to start hiking again.

body: Jan 7th I had lapidus surgery. For the last few weeks I have been feeling frustrated with swelling and pain it was mostly on the inside of my foot then it went to top of my foot. Has happened ever since I started wearing sneakers so I wear my boot. So I went the surgeon today and he did X-rays. It shows my hard ware on the top is pushing away it is thankfully still in place and the plate is fused and healing well but he said he will keep an eye on it.
Uh… did anyone else wear a toe spacer for a while and as a result get a pain in the middle top of the foot? I feel like I'm trading one issue for another.

body: Just talk to doc, I've decided to do PRP. Anyone done this I'm a chicken
comments: Don't waste your money.
I have and it's not effective at all!!
Tape your feet with zinc oxide tape
My son is college pitcher and had it done in his shoulder without success, but I think it just depends. He's had teammates have success. Give it a try!
ing.. in the same boat!
I've had this done on my left foot for Plantar Fasciitis and it was fantastic! Had it done in 2019 and to this day no issue with PF in that foot.
I done this for my elbow helped for a year or so. I have PF have for years have tried everything except that or surgery nothing works

The tendons and ligaments around my wife's hip was causing her very bad pain. She did everything the doctor recommended and nothing helped. He told her that a PRP injection has helped some but not everyone. She decided to try it. 3 weeks later the pain began to subside and now she's pain free.
A friend did it for an elbow thing. Said he's pain free and very happy with PRP.
Evidence shows that progressive strengthening of the foot and other relevant muscles is the most effective way to treat PF, and prevent it from ever returning.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Does the treadmill make anyone else's pf flare up and burn? I want to exercise but I suffer for days if I push myself. What other exercises is good that won't make pf flare up?
comments: Yes. My doctor said no treadmill. I do the exercise bike and yoga

The treadmill is one of the worst things that you can be on because every single solitary step is exactly the same one right after the other. The alternative would be either a really good exercise bike or what I think is the best of the best which is an elliptical.
At physical therapy I would start on a bike for 5 minutes to get the blood moving. I usually stayed on it longer
Elliptical, NuStep, Bike, Swimming, weights, Rower.
Yep, switched to the bike for a year. Hated it. But necessary. I bought my own seat to bring to the gym
I had to switch to swimming
Treadmill use was one of the reasons I have pf. It's very bad for pf. Elliptical trainer has been my go to
An incline over 3 will do it.
Unfortunately treadmills don't have the same force absorption as the ground does. I would avoid the treadmill until your PF subsides.
I was thinking of trying a rowing machine. I heard it is great exercise! Anyone tried rowing with pf?

U had only inflammation?
How much time soaking
How much of the Epsom salts, apple cider vinegar, and tee tree oils do you use? How long do you soak them?
Yes! I did this as well and it helped a Lot! After being in pain n barely walking for 6 months or so, my feet have been great for past 3 months!
Hot Epson water is the only thing that relieved my pain.
Everybody talks about their heel hurting, but mine is sore right in the middle of my foot so do you all think that is PF?
I'm gonna try this.
I do the graston technique on my feet and calves after soaking mine in a very hot Epsom salt bath. TenJet got me to the 90% mark and this got me to the 100% mark.
Epsom salt works for mine

Yes, that is the last hold out for pain for me. I have had shots, multiple rounds of PT and the tenex procedure and that is the pain that remains no matter what I do.
Yes, have had heel spurs. The only thing you can do for them is put something like Dr. Scholl half size heel pads in the heel area of your shoes.
I got professional massages after 3 rounds of cortisone shots. Tell them you have PF. Mine is gone. Lived with it for 5 years.

body: Hi everyone, Had foot nail fungus for 17 years now. I'm pretty positive I caught it with my mom's contaminated nail polish and clippers. I wasn't aware. It started on my left big toe and then it spread like wildfire to the other. I also had AF and my feet were itchy. So never took medical care because I was ashamed. I went years without exposing my feet. I was afraid of vacations and yoga classes. Anything that involves exposing my feet. I'm 33 and I said no more to this embarrassment. I have insurance and I decided to see a good Foot and ankle doctor. She took clipping sent it to a lab and then said Terbinafine is the answer for 12 weeks.

Just a note for those considering oral meds, I didn't have a single side effect taking it for 3 months and my liver wasn't affected at all, it stayed normal. I am religious so I don't drink alcohol or coffee and never have done drugs so my liver is likely better than most. I did have to take pain meds on my period which I was worried about but still no side effects. My nail fungus was mild but had lasted over 7 years.
Which home remedies have you tried, and did they actually work for you?
comments: Vicks worked for me and many others
Nonyx nail gel, dettol in your washing. Regularly filing and cleaning out nail. Epsom salts and apple cider vinegar foot soaks.

I've had this going on for 5 years plus, I've tried everything and nothing worked so I hear every one talking about Vicks vapor rub and gave it a shot. U have to get rid of the nail by grinding it down or clipping it off and I use peroxide in warm water, put my feet in and soak about 10 min then put on Vicks and socks and go to bed, I can tell it's working!

I was on Reddit awhile back trying to look at new ways and someone posted Betadine/iodine 10% solution. You have to cut the nails back really short and then file them until they are a bit porous and then apply twice a day for a few months maybe longer depending on level of infection.
None
Vinegar. 5 toes had it. It's taken Almost 2 years. 4 toes clear... big toe close. But better than anything else I've tried in 15 years including meds.. prescriptions.. Vicks... nothing else worked.

I've tried so many, some worked a little, but nothing cured it until I finally just took terbinafine. I partially procrastinate because the topical version did nothing, but the oral medication worked.
Vicks works if you apply a nice amount and cover it with a band-Aid.

body: I'm scheduled for surgery this Saturday. I've been reading through posts to get an idea of how I'm going to feel/manage. I like to think I push pain to one side and just get on with things however I know that I'll definitely need rest and elevation. I've given myself a 2 week schedule to switch off. I'm office based and plan to go back to work, sedentary after 2 weeks. Am I being unrealistic? How was your pain in the first few days? How long until weight bearing? Did it drive you crazy doing nothing?
comments: What procedure are you having? I had a lapidus last year and worked from home for 15 weeks. I am 10 weeks post op on my second lapidus and still home.
I had Lapidus along with osteotomy on three toes. I only needed pain meds the first two days. I work from home and was able to return to work the second week. I did elevate while sitting at my desk.
My surgery was 2/25. My pain was manageable as I kept on top of it. The first few days I alternated between pain meds and Tylenol and then after about 5 days I only took Tylenol.
I managed 4 days taking my pain medication religiously before I had to get up and move about and it was the best decision I made even with pins in my toes.
It all depends on your non weight bearing length of time, if you are in plaster or not. I'm 10 days post op in plaster NWB for 6 weeks.

It depends on the surgery you are having and whether you are able to be weight bearing. I had lapiplasty which was pretty invasive and I just started walking at 8 weeks, driving at 9 because it was the right foot.
It depends on the procedure. I had a chevron osteotomy and I had my foot wrapped for 4 weeks and was advised to elevate for that entire time.
I took two weeks off work and spent that time resting, reading, binged one series, and adjusting to the lack of mobility as an active person. Knee scooter was a lifesaver.
First foot I had to be carried to bathroom for days, and was on pain meds for 5 weeks. Second foot walked from start and can take the dog out from week 2.
I had lapiplasty. I could walk using my heel in a boot in a few weeks. The pain only lasted about 5 days.
