Any recommendations for a narrow slipper or slide to wear in the house? I wear a sz 10.5 narrow. While I know a wider toe box is best, many slides are too wide for me in my sz- they literally fly off my feet when trying to walk. I prefer not to wear shoes or sneakers inside as it's too hot.

body: Looking for anyone's experience with this product or with tea tree oil for getting rid of toenail fungus. Has either product worked for you? Im desperate
Castor oil, is it worth trying? If you use this, how are you applying it, and does it have to be the organic one?

Does anyone else find relief with Oofos slides? My pain was manageable until I purchased these slides. That's when I noticed that when I wore them, I felt much better BUT overall the pain started increasing. I had to stop going to the gym even. I still wear them because they are the only slides that are so comfortable——but I'm wondering if they are making the healing process slower.
comments: All I wear are Oofos and sneakers

My PF is starting to disappear. However, my outer ankle on my bad foot has been a problem. I'm sure it's because I have been limping and walking improperly for so long. My Oofos seem to aggravate my ankle situation.
They are so comfortable but put my PF into a stretched position that flares mine.

I wore mine when my pain was at its worst. As my pain improved, while wearing regular shoes, after I lost some weight and was on anti inflammatories I didn't wear them as much. I, too, noticed they were sometimes adding to my pain after wearing them.

So a few orthopedic surgeons and podiatrists tell me that OOFOS are exactly what they say they are. RECOVERY shoes. They said they are meant to be worn for painful days, not EVERYDAY. Not supportive enough, and too soft and pliable. Pretty much they intensify your problem if worn daily.
Nah they are rubbish, don't get the comments.
Its not wise to wear just one type of shoe for days. You need to swap out. You need to switch shoes out daily, every couple days - your feet will tell you.
I find them only good when I'm extremely inflamed and just for a short time
Made me worse
If it's painful, how is it comfortable to you?

body: I wanted to share this for anyone in here who feels like there's no way out of plantar fasciitis right now. It can get better. But it takes time, consistency, and patience. I've been dealing with it since January 2025. Like a lot of people, I tried to push through it at first - kept running until around August when it finally stopped me in my tracks and had me limping.
comments: This is really positive; thanks for sharing
May I ask how you do the calf stretches and what runners were you using along with the Oofos?
Thank you for the encouraging words! This all seems so hopeless sometimes. Did you have setbacks along your recovery journey?
Never underestimate the power of the big toe!!!
I'm finally glad to see someone with results after strengthening the foot. Everyone seems to stretch but not strength train. I'm doing things similar to you. I am also a runner.
This really made me feel better! I think a lot of my issues stem from tight calves and weakness in my glutes, calves and hips.
Thank you for sharing. Will try!
Thanks so much for the detailed guidance. You have me feeling hopeful.
Good advice. It's a marathon not a sprint. This developed over time and requires time to heal with proper exercise and treatment.
Thanks so much for the ideas and mainly the hope!

body: Anyone dealt with tailors bunion.? I can feel the bone just a little down from my little toe & my little toe hurts on the side .. any at home relief ?

Tailor's bunions tend to get callouses, which hurt. Callouses are prone to warts. If you get all 3 together you need to see a GOOD podiatrist. A wart can look like a callous and you think it's just a callous.
Get it fixed. My tailors bunion was so painful after working on my feet all day. Glad I did. Easy surgery
I use toe spreaders and a wide toe box shoe like Altra running shoes. Then use some ibuprofen to knock down inflammation. It then goes away, takes couple weeks but works.

body: I am incredibly frustrated and depressed about my feet. I feel like no one even knows what's wrong with them. Going on 7 years battling pain in the Tops of my feet. Resting them only makes them better until I start using them again. I've tried gabapentin (didn't work plus I hated how it made me feel). I've tried several pain meds that barely help, but also can't be on forever. Had xrays and even two emgs (testing nerves).
comments: Have your heel fat pads ever been measured? Heel fat pad atrophy is a common yet VERY underdiagnosed condition
Same sinking boat. Following for advice. Quite literally feel your pain. I'm only 50

I took a recommendation of using B6 vitamin. I'm on day four using B6 and B12. Day three I felt a difference and today my feet don't feel so thick/inflamed/sore. On second day I also started watching how many oxalates I consumed (spinach, cashews, nuts, parsley etc) as that can be a cause of sore feet too.
I made a cayenne pepper balm for my husband's feet. Really does work on the pain. Recipes online

I had Rheumatoid Arthritis Bone Spurs Heel Spurs Plantar Fasciitis Haglunds Deformity Tendonitis heel Spurs Gout I could barely walk and always in pain I took all the meds and did all the therapy. Four years later using canes knee scooter. I had more tests and needed a Parathyroidectomy.
Yes!!! Four years here of similar pain. I've recently started the new Stemwave Therapy and have been pain free, it's literally a miracle!!
I often stand up and it feels like all the bones are crunching together across the tops of my feet. I can't wear laces in trainers anymore. Walking is unbearable after about 7-10 mins.

body: Just Got surgery last Monday the 18th pain was very manageable with meds. I took oxy for 3-4 days then just used Motrin and nerve blocker. I take Calcium K2 and D3 daily they all work together to get into system and to bones and joints for healing. I did step on foot yesterday with a little discomfort after uhhhh big toe gets swollen when I'm up too long I washed my hair today and showered guess that was bit too much. I sleep with leg elevated.

Not gonna lie, this pain has been some of the hardest I've ever had to deal with. I go for my dressing change today and am excited but also nervous to find out if it's healing the way it should be. I'm grateful because my doctor prescribed more strong pain meds so I'm relieved about that.

Last Wednesday I had my MIS surgery, the first two nights were very painful but by my follow up 7 days later I was feeling pretty good. (Still icing and elevating a lot). Unfortunately x-rays showed one of the screws had become loose so I had another surgery this morning. The pain is not so tolerable. I'll say in advance that I think everyone has different needs depending on what the structural issue is (flat feet vs high arches). Fit Flops have been the best for me. Changed my life honestly. I wear their flip flop type shoes, sneakers and boots.
I have a rec for one of each - true flip flip: Archie's (from Australia) and a Havaiana's slide (from Brazil). Have walked miles unplanned in each, including in the rain, mud, and forest trails.
I just bought some Skechers Slip-in ones that are super comfy too. Your best bet is to try on a whole bunch to find which feel best for you!
These look decent, are very comfortable, and have clinical arch support. PowerStep Archwear Women's Arch Supporting Thong Sandal.
I think birks are cute! They're my go to summer sandal

body: Hello, I finally got in to see orthopedic surgeon for my bunion. I’m extremely flat footed and have overpronation issues. He said he would need to do total foot reconstruction, build an arch, fix my bunion and lengthen a muscle in my calf. Has anyone ever gone through this?

body:
I need to get something off my chest. Here's my feet. I don't necessarily have wide feet, but I have very flat feet. Due to the wrong footwear as a child (too small or too wide, long story short), they started to grow in a fan shape. The photo with the burned feet was taken when I was 17. I neglected my feet...especially in my twenties. By putting my feet in unsuitable shoes, I developed bunions. I'm trying to accept them, but I find it very difficult. I have insoles from the orthopedist, those blue ones, and they're absolutely useless...they don't work. At the specialty store that also fits insoles, they said I need a completely different kind of insole, much deeper and thicker at the forefoot. Now there's almost no insole there...very unusual. I'm fed up with it. To be completely honest, the doctor already told me I'm not suitable for insoles and that I just need surgery...I'd prefer surgery right away for my flat feet. Does anyone have experience with this? Possibly one of these things: I have really difficult feet, and I hardly notice I'm wearing shoes anymore. I'm really scared of the surgery, to be honest, and I'm trying to accept it at the moment, but finding shoes, etc., is just really difficult. I actually have a pretty slim heel. Sorry for the long story, I hope someone hears me. I'm in pain 24/7
Because of these
complaints, I also often have back and knee pain, which makes proper movement like running impossible. Thank you for reading
comments: I've had foot surgery twice and deeply regret having the first one. I had a Weil Osteotomy for a plantar plate tear and a bunionectomy that was undercorrected. I went to a surgeon that was overly confident. I had the second surgery to remove scar tissue and screws removed. My foot still hurts. You may feel worse after surgery. I read too many positive reviews and wish I would have done more research. I wish I would have gone to an orthopedic surgeon vs a podiatrist from the start. Get another opinion. Try other inserts. I'll send some links to inserts I like. I've had a hip replaced at 41 and shoulder surgery. Foot surgery has been the hardest! I didn't have passion in my bunions, but did the bunion because my big toe was pushing my second toe to the right. My surgeon said it was causing strain on my second toe. Post surgery care was crap and my foot was weak, thus causing more issues.

Your bunions don't look bad enough for surgery, in my opinion. I've had bunion surgery on both feet aroundc11 or 12 years ago. I'm also flat footed and have hammer toes. The surgery made my feet look better but my left foot still had a small bunion. Now, my left foot, especially hurts after only walking short distances. I plan to have surgery again in November to correct the bunion and hammer toes. From what my surgeon says, there is a new procedure that's less invasive. It's called Prostep bunion surgery
conditions_mentioned: bunion, flat feet, hammer toe, plantar plate tear

I’ve been dealing with plantar fasciitis in both feet for about 14 months now, and it’s honestly exhausting. It came out of nowhere one day while I was walking outside, and it just hasn’t gone
It used to be only when I woukd stand but I also notice it aches now too (I do stand a few hours at work, so that doesn’t help). I’ve tried a lot—Brooks sneakers, custom orthotics (which I actually feel might be making things worse), PT, massages, stretching… but nothing seems to fully fix it. I’ll admit I haven’t been the most consistent with stretching, but still, it’s frustrating not seeing real improvement.
Cortisone shots help for a bit, but the pain always comes back. I’ve also noticed my calves are extremely tight—they’re so sore to the touch. I try foam rolling and stretching them.. foam rolling os very painful.
I’ve started catching myself walking more on the front of my foot to avoid heel pain, which I know probably isn’t helping either.
If anyone has dealt with this or has advice, I’m all ears. I’m so tired of being in pain and just want to feel normal again. This has been beyond frustrating
comments: This sounds like I wrote this

I was the same until I found out how the pro athletes get rid of it (I had never heard of a pro footballer being out with PF for longer than a few weeks). That led me to Professor Keith Baar’s research and how we can only heal the injury properly by overcoming Stress Shielding (which is like limping but for connective tissue fibres). When I got all the research together and understood what I need to do it took me 6-7 weeks to get pain free (doing 10 minutes of easy exercise twice per day). If you want more information let me know, good luck

Has anyone actually gotten rid of plantar fasciitis just from stretching? I’ve spent hundreds of dollars on chiropractors, massages, inserts, supportive shoes, foam rollers, foot rollers, and physical therapy. I have it in both feet, and cortisone shots only helped one foot. But only last 3 minths..I’ve been dealing with this pain since February and I’m beyond frustrated and desperate for relief. It just won’t go away. I just want to be able to run at the gym and do cardio again without my feet burning

Wells Same here I’m so upset that I can’t walk my 3-4 miles everyday because my steroid shot didn’t work this time and I do stretching, icing and everything you can think of but it’s getting worse everyday. I don’t know what to do. I’ve had it for 2 years now!

You have to break up the scar tissue in the muscle tissue of the calves and legs. No amount of stretching will help until you break up those adhesions. Go to Dr. Angela Walk's YouTube channel and follow her at-home protocol. Only thing that gets at the root causes. After 5 years of chronic pain, someone in this group posted about this. I'm so grateful! There is hope!

Just wanted to share my experience! I had plantar fasciitis in both feet. I bought Brooks shoes and had custom inserts. It felt a little better, but I was still in so much pain. I got a cortisone shot in both feet — it helped the right one, and I haven’t had pain come back there since. The left foot, I had two shots, and they didn’t do anything. I tried foot baths, Epsom salt, physical therapy, chiropractic care, stretches, foam roll

Thank you for sharing. To your experience, does a single session make a difference? Specific type of massage? Do you plan on going weekly / monthly? I’m asking because I had a few and they felt great but didn’t provide real relief.
Many thanks for your post and I completely agree. You need to work on your entire leg.

Has anybody who’s had plantar fasciitis gotten cortisone shots and had it go away for good? I have it in BOTH feet and got cortisone shots, but I feel it coming back and it’s only been a month. I bought good shoes and have custom inserts, but I don’t know what else to do.
Bales I saw where a lot of people on this page recommended crocs. I tried them last week....game changer!

Muse 1st shot lasted 2+ years. It was given by an older, more experienced doctor. He retired and the new Dr gave me an injection, but seemed more interested in selling me high $$ orthotics and talking about surgery. I joined hoping to learn more

suffered for years. I thought the pain was actually from my toenail and I kept telling podiatrists that it was so bad that I just wanted them to remove the nail permanently. body: I've had plantar fasciitis off and on for over two years. Saw my podiatrist today and learned I now have developed achilles tendonitis and the beginning stage of a neuroma in this same foot. Anyone else in the same boat? Other than the prescribed meloxicam and stretches, do you have any tips?
comments: I had surgery after 15 YEARS of PF!! Now I’m pain free!!
Night splints... please share pros and cons and if I should be wearing them. Thanks so much.

You don’t need to wear them. They will keep you in pain for longer. There is nothing wrong with your foot being pointed down or up while you sleep. In the morning do the ABCs on your foot and stretch if needed.
Do not wear them Keep a pillow under your ankles to keep from straining feet during sleep. Immediately stretch on side of bed before taking first step.
For those taking magnesium glycinate... what time of day is it best to take it? Any adverse side effects I should expect?
Seymour I take it before bed. It’s supposed to help your muscles relax. I have had zero side effects. I almost feel like I sleep better when I take it.
Definitely b4 bed as it helps with sleep and relaxing your body.
Does the injection hurt
would stop icing now anyway as ice for PF isn’t the best way to solve this issue.

body: Hi - i have PF and a neuroma. I have done stretching, ice, splints, Vibit,etc. for 5weeks. No real improvement. I also have Hoka Giralto(?), Arachi, NB 1080s - wonder if these have made worse. Any viable other treatment options before shots that have worked for you? Thanks
comments: Anyone have insight into whether you should /can do gentle walking with PF? If so, do you walk until twinging or stop before. I am also doing stretching, ice, splint, etc
I reduced my walking for a few months but then I figured that my feet were getting generally unconditioned which can’t be good, particularly if you get walking again too quickly.

You can walk but keep it minimal. Assessing 24 hours later to see if PF symptoms have increased. If they haven't then walked the same again for another few days in a row. If everything is ok then consider increasing duration a bit. An inspiration of mine was a guy who walked 5 minutes a day. After 2 weeks was PF symptoms not increasing he increased walking by 5 minutes. He rinsed and repeated until he was walking an hour a day. Which from memory equates to 6 months of walking. He healed through slow and progressive loading while not re-injuring. I healed through slow and progressive high-loading. It means I achieve the same result but in 1.5 months as I was increasing load. I also did my regime once every two days, resting on the 2nd

body:
Today renewed my belief that PF and Mortons Neuroma are manageable. I've had definite improvement from foot and leg strengthening and stretching plus Altras and toe socks but have found it hard going while holidaying in Vietnam. Yesterday I had a massage that included the lovely Vietnamese lady turning me into a pretzel with some great stretches and working through my leg from hip to toes extensively including quads (often missed in massages). Today I had the best day on my feet in 6 months solid. I only cramped up in my foot once after about 15000 steps and even that released fairly easily.
This has reassured me that if we work the kinks out of our bodies as a larger system the pain can be significantly reduced or stop.
comments: Claudia Hillstrom Great news, as I have suffered from both. I am relatively comfortable on my feet most days, but I do need to do more in strengthening and stretching. I too have found that massage has helped me to heal. I want to work toward barefoot shoes as I think that will be key to my feet getting stronger. Curious, are you wearing toe socks inside your Altras?

After being up and down with PF for several months and not being able to get it under control with my physio I went to a podiatrist who says I also have Morton's Neuroma (inflammation of the nerve between 2nd and 3rd toes) which is why my experience with PF hasn't quite fit the mold. Podiatrist has prescribed anti inflammatories and, of course, custom orthotics Just wondering if anyone else has the same condition and has any tips for how they've managed it.

I suffered with Morton's Neuroma for years both feet. I also had bunions. They didn't hurt but my big toe started to rotate . Had surgery to fix bunions and the neuromas. Finally healed and now years later I have PF
Yvonne Wood I had Morton’s neuroma and had cryosurgery which removed it.

But I'm al

I am in agony by about 5p.m.
Not even walking very far. Been diagnosed with a bunion and years ago with collapsed metatarsals...later told this was probably wrong.
Bunion in one foot, pain and numbness in both, Been checked for Peripheral Neuropathy. All supposed to be normal apart from bunion.

Big toe nerve pain?
I have a small bunion on my left foot which causes me a lot of foot pain. I went and saw a podiatrist when the outer edge of my big toe became numb and my pain got worse. He told me my bunion was too small to be causing pain and said all my bones are shifting to the left which is a sign of something else going on. He said he couldn’t even correct the bunion. He wanted me to have a nerve conduction study done first. I got one and it’s normal.
Anyone else have the toe nerve numbness? I also have excruciating pain when my big toe is hit on the outer top. It almost feels like an ingrown nail but it’s not, just some nerve I guess.
comments: Did he have x-rays taken? He can't know what your foot looks like inside. I would demand x-rays first. Also, my back causes what I thought was neuropathy pain. I have a displaced nerve root. If your insurance pays for it, get a second opinion. It could be too early to correct the bunion. My podiatrist told me it have to be over a 9 degree variance off. Mine was 27 and I am not joking. I put it off that long after I saw a podiatrist when I was younger who never took x-rays and didn't do surgery. I figured I would just hear the same. My primary care doctor said I needed to see again so I did and got a big difference when I went this time. I am a week out from surgery. I wish I had gone in earlier than waiting from age 30ish to age 61.

Support Group(: Hi, new here. I had pain under my big toe for months and one day I noticed part of my toe was numb so I went to urgent care and they told me I had a bunion with a cyst. My podiatrist appt is tomorrow. I had no idea what a bunion was and I’m still a bit shocked. I bought new shoes, a tube of voltaren, and those toe separator things. They don’t seem to help. Actually, the toe separator makes my nerve pain worse. It feels like there’s hot water running on my toes. I tried to sleep with it last night and today the bottom of my foot right under the ball is so sore like I hiked in flats or something. I did buy a metatarsal sleeve that actually does feel better but my new shoes, which I bought in a size up, still don’t have enough toe room for me to wear it so my toe rubbed against the side and hurts now. This is not fun! Anyone else have issues with not being able to wear the bunion supports?

Yes, the toe separators that I have seem to cut the circulation and cause some of my toes to go numb. I cannot sleep with them. Hopefully someone will weigh in with good toe separator advice, there are good ones I think, I’ve seen comments on this group where people like them — maybe from Gaithappens? Gaithappens have an Instagram account worth following. Another account to check is thefootcollective Consider Altra and Topo running shoes for toe splay space. Whatever the brand though, look for natural foot shape footwear— wearing the running shoes with toe box space is best. Those two brands, Altra and Topo, are well known for being foot shaped. I ordered online and the returns policies seem good. I now have one pair of each of those brands. Another thing that I want to learn to do is to use physio tape to tape my bunions. I wonder if it may be an alternative to using the toe separators? I’ve seen videos that may help to learn the technique. I’ve had physiotherapists tape my feet in the past… oh that’s another on the to-do list: go to physiotherapy. We all have weak bits that have contributed (or caused) the foot issues. Stopping progression or even reversing some of it, depends on strength and identifying if there is a condition like hypermobility. Our muscles are what hold us up, it’s all connected, even shoulder and hip positions affect how we land on our feet (obviously knees and ankles too) and glute strength is key. Learn your weaknesses. Good luck, managing bunions is a journey.
conditions_mentioned: bunion, foot pain, ingrown toenail, nerve damage, neuropathy, numbness

body:
Hello all! I had surgery on August 2, 2024. I’ve been going through a lot of nerve pain/damage, swelling and CONSTANT pain in my toe. I also have lost about 75% of feeling in my toe as well. I had a bunionectomy along with the surgeon setting/breaking my toe and placing in two screws to hold the toe in place. I also had Os trigonum surgery done to the back part of my foot as well.
If anyone gets this far, does anyone know any over the counter things I could use for pain and nerve pain? I’m losing insurance at the beginning of the year and I will no longer be able to see my doctor for support. So I was wondering if anyone had any things they’ve used that actually helps with pain? I also can not take NSAIDs as I am also a bariatric patient. Thank you!!
comments: I'm no doctor or specialist but I've researched extensively over the past 6 years and in this time I have designed a flip flop/ thongs against bunions. Sorry not on the market yet. What I'm about to say is based on your doctor and that the operation went well ? Spread your toes and actuate them on the floor for 2 minutes 3 times with a rest in between. Massage and manipulate your toes and feet before bed. By a product called CorrectToes and a pair of Barefoot shoes. A full explanation is in a video called "How to fix bunions in 5 steps" by Barefoot Strength This guy Chris is very good and explains very well.

Hi everyone.
Former athlete here.
At the age of 20, I started experiencing pain in my right big toe, and around the same time I developed chronic muscle tightness in my calves. Over the years, this tightness gradually spread to my thighs, glutes, and lower back, and also to muscle tightness around the shoulder blades and chest (not sharp pain, more of a constant tight feeling).
Surgery on rt foot for Tailor’s bunion and hammer toe (4th) on Tuesday. Nervous but excited to FINALLY deal with this after a lifetime of pain that has gotten worse in recent years.

Has anyone had surgery to remove Tailor’s bunions? I’m curious about the recovery. I work on my feet and am wondering if the recovery takes a long time and if you had full recovery. Is there swelling long term? How long did you have to be off work?

body:
This Shoe Mistake Makes Plantar Fasciitis Worse
If you’ve bought multiple pairs of “supportive,” extra-cushioned shoes hoping this next pair would finally fix your plantar fasciitis…
You’re not alone.
I talk to people every single week who’ve done the exact same thing.
They were told that the softer, squishier, more supportive the shoe, the safer it is for your feet.
But, If those shoes were going to heal your plantar fasciitis, wouldn’t they have worked by now?
Over time, those shoes do the opposite of what you want.
They restrict natural foot movement
They reduce muscle activity
And they slowly make your feet weaker
And weak feet don’t heal.
Most people don’t realize that supportive shoes and orthotics might give you temporary relief, but they don’t fix the root cause.
Real correction comes from restoring how your foot was designed to function:
Shoes that allow your toes to spread and move naturally
Daily mobility work to release tight fascia and calves
Progressive strengthening of your feet and lower legs
I break this down in my latest YouTube video: Cushioned Shoes Are Making Your Plantar Fasciitis Worse
In this video, I’ll show you why supportive shoes can keep you stuck, and what to look for in a better shoe
Bottom line: Strength + mobility = lasting relief
All my best (and then some),
Dr. Angela
comments: Sharp Dr Angela Walk is great! Started following her advice a couple months ago, now I'm walking over a mile a day, and building on that. Couldn't walk at all before. Buying the right shoes was a game changer. I bought Altra Escalantes
Sharpe Toe yoga and foot strength exercises!!!!

This is an email I regularly receive from Dr. Angela from YouTube…she has saved me MONTHS of pain… 3 Mistakes That Trigger Flare-Ups If your plantar fasciitis has flared up again, that's usually not by mistake. I know that feels frustrating. You were making progress. Things were starting to feel better. And then, out of nowhere, it’s back. Things to consider: Flare-ups are rarely random. In most cases, your foot was simply asked to do more than it was ready for. And I see the same 3 mistakes over and over again… 1. You tested the waters with your “normal” shoes Things were improving, so you thought it might be safe. You slipped back into those cute shoes sitting in your closet , but they still have the same damaging features: narrow toe box, elevated heel, too much support. Your foot wasn’t ready for that yet. And it let you know. 2. You ramped up your activity too fast Less pain makes you feel like you can do more. So you walk longer, stand more, maybe add workouts back in, but your foot hasn’t rebuilt the strength to handle that load yet. So it pushes back. 3. You got a little inconsistent with the program You skipped a few days, maybe more. Figured it wouldn’t make a big difference. Staying consistent with your strength and mobility work is everything. This is what actually rebuilds your foot. This is what increases your capacity. Without it, your foot stays vulnerable. Keep this in mind. Feeling better isn’t the same as being better. Pain going down doesn’t mean your foot is fully healed. It just means it’s tolerating what you’re currently asking of it. Real recovery happens when your foot is strong enough to handle more, without flaring up. So if you’ve had a setback recently, Don’t panic. Just get back on track. Build your strength. Progress gradually. Stay consistent. That’s how this gets better… Dr. Angela
Hi what's the strength and mobility exercise please
How do you get on her emails?

I am 11 days out from bunion surgery correction along with a gastroc slide and osteotomy on first & second toe. I have been elevating and icing as much as I can throughout the day. Off the narcotics, but using Tylenol for pain relief as needed.
When seated for a few minutes the foot starts to tingle, then feels heavy & then feels like it is in a vise. Once this happens it is hard to reverse. I have learned to NOT ignore the tingling & to elevate as soon as I feel it coming on.
Just curious for about how long these type of sensations might last? I know healing is different for everyone and we each have variations on surgery, but I wasn’t expecting to have such a problem with sitting up for more 15 minutes at this point or are my expectations just way off
! See less
comments: Lasts for weeks until I was actually moving around more ( 6/7 weeks ) feeling each time I put my foot down , completely normal but a weird sensation for sure . Blood rushing back down to your foot ..and nerves waking up
Did hoka shoes help or make your plantar fasciitis worse

I tried EVERYTHING to heal my plantar fasciitis. I stopped all exercise on it, I had shockwave therapy, I had cortisone injections, I did taping, I had custom insoles, I had smart feet insoles, I had physical therapy, I did the ibuprofen and the icing and the stretching... Then I bought HOKA and started wearing them exclusively and started walking a mile a day and my plantar fasciitis went away. I won't wear any other brand of shoe for the fear of getting plantar fasciitis back I suffered with it for 3 years before it finally went away with the HOKA.
My podiatrist said my Hokas were too soft and may have been part of the reason I got PF! He recommended Brooks and Vionics.

comments: I tried EVERYTHING to heal my plantar fasciitis. I stopped all exercise on it, I had shockwave therapy, I had cortisone injections, I did taping, I had custom insoles, I had smart feet insoles, I had physical therapy, I did the ibuprofen and the icing and the stretching... Then I bought HOKA and started wearing them exclusively and started walking a mile a day and my plantar fasciitis went away. I won't wear any other brand of shoe for the fear of getting plantar fasciitis back I suffered with it for 3 years before it finally went away with the HOKA.

I am flatfoot and have PF...my Podiatrist suggested insoles, NSAID cream and calf exercises...Its been about 2 weeks and those suggestions have helped however, I still get a burning sensation on the bottom of my big toes.
She also suggested a 2500 mg CBD cream which I have yet to buy...Has anyone had any luck with CBD cream for PF symptoms?

Hello all!
I saw a new podiatrist last Monday 4/13.
He suggested PowerStep inserts, prescribed a 7 day course of prednisone, calf stretches 2x a day and meloxicam that I start today.
I FINALLY HAVE SOME RELIEF! I’m not 100% better but so much better than I was! I pray the rest of you find relief as well!

Consultant really happy with my foot. No more bandage just single dressing and toe splint for next 4 weeks. Still with shoe and crutches.
So 13 days post op on my right foot...
The consultant was really happy with my foot. No more bandage just single dressing and toe splint for next 4 weeks. Still with shoe and crutches.
Some bruising but not as much as I thought. Dressing can be changed weekly. Stitches will dissolve. He snipped a couple.
Follow up with x-ray in 4 weeks.
Asked about my other foot. He said he can review this with me and keep me on his list so I don't need another referral from GP. Would do 4-6 mths after right foot so I'll definitely consider it I think?!!
He seemed pleased with how it was so that's good

Jenn Chladil I saw my podiatrist on Monday. I am allowed full use of my foot now, with a shoe on, and found out something I wanted to share with you all.
The reason the doctors have you putting weight on your foot so early, even with all the swelling that comes with it etc, is to prevent disuse osteopenia. It is where the bone minerals start to break down because your body tells your foot it isn't being used so no need to rebuild bone.
How did I find this out? Because my surgeon's PA told me not to put weight on my foot at four weeks. I didn't see my podiatrist until Monday (7 weeks out) and had x-rays taken. When he entered the room and saw my rollator and boot he asked why I was still using them. I told him what the PA told me. He looked at my husband who acknowledged that yes she said that. The podiatrist said, "Well that explains your x-rays." I thought my foot wasn't healing. I began to freak out. He brought up the x-rays and showed me the bone is healing fine but it looked kind of cloudy/dark on the x-ray over all. He told me it is disuse osteopenia. He said to start walking on that foot immediately with the boot on for a week and then switch to a tennis shoe.
If you have been told by someone other than your podiatrist not to put weight on your foot and you are over 4 weeks out, contact the podiatrist directly to find out if you should or shouldn't be using your foot. It is a normal part of being in a cast or boot and is completely reversable if is it just from disuse.
I was told my next set of x-rays in 6 weeks should show it has cleared up. I swear medical people need to communicate with each other more. See less
comments: Carrie Miller Haas Wow. I wish I had known this earlier. I was in a boot for 12 weeks. X-rays were showing possible non-union because of that cloudy line where bone was trying to grow back together. I’ve been walking for a month now with a shoe, but have to use a Bone Stimulator for 3 months because I’m “behind”. Thank you for sharing!

Jenn Chladil, thank goodness you’ve caught it early enough. You are getting so close.Yay! Yes, definitely issues with communication. You know I’ve had all kinds of issues, and pretty sure I will have lasting mobility issues with that toe. But, in my case my foot is the least of my worries.

I wanted to share something that’s been affecting me for a while. Living with toenail fungus might seem like a small thing to other people, but for me it’s actually impacted a lot of parts of my daily life.
I’ve felt embarrassed to show my feet, wear sandals, go to the beach, things like that. And honestly, one of the hardest parts is trying treatments and not seeing results as quickly as I hoped.
I’m curious if anyone else here fee
Boric acid works! Try it.
What does this look like? My doctor said it might be cancer

Almost healed nail
I’ve had nail fungus on my big toe for years. I tried every cure there is except for oral medication. Nothing worked. Finally I started putting hexane free, cold pressed, organic castor oil on my nail all day and all night with a bandaid on. I applied it once in the morning with a bandaid and once at night. My nail was horrible before and now it’s almost clear (it’s been about 6 months). It still needs to grow in on the sides a bit better but it’s clear!

I’ve had several ask me for the routine that I used to cure my fungus. It took a year of following this routine daily to grow them out.
I soaked in white iodine for 1 minute per toe. I put the iodine in 2” glass bowls I purchased on Amazon. (Put the bowls in a disposable casserole pan because they spill).
I soaked in it twice per day for one week, then once per day for another week, then go to three times a week, then two times a week and then once per week until it’s grow

I searched group for Jublia before posting. Have had fungus on one nail for years. Tried laser therapy twice. And a clear fungus polish daily. Along with all the home remedies. Still a little fungus left and doctor recommended Jublia. It goes on once a day then remove with polish remover after 7 days. The side effects listed with brochure has me paranoid. Anyone have experience with it?

comments: You can go to the gym, but keep it low impact to avoid flare-ups from Plantar Fasciitis and Gout. **Best options:** Stationary bike Swimming (best if available) Machines: chest press, lat pulldown, seated row Leg machines (light only):** leg press (low weight) leg curl leg extension Avoid:** running jumping stair climber Start slow and increase gradually.

comments: Anonymous member 910 Comp and bonuses sound attractive in the beginning. She needs to ask about VRC, your renewal commission base 4.5% and you have to reach certain metrics to earn full comp. If you are not quoting home/auto bundles over a certain % they restrict your home bind ability. Policy cancellations are restricted and customer has to call Farmers retention team to cancel. This is all in Texas. Your state may be different.
All the Farmer’s agents I know are ex-Farmers agents.

(: Jenn Chladil Hello fellow "Did I really do this?", I had a new thing happen tonight. I have been putting an elastic bandage on my foot since I am able to walk now. It helps with the swelling. I took it off tonight because I wanted to lotion my foot. Holy cow! It looked like a snake shedding its skin!! I immediately googled to see if the internet could tell me if this was normal. IT IS!! Who knew! When your foot is swollen for a while, the skin stretches and as your foot heals, it sheds that old skin. This is healthy and completely normal. It means you are healing. I have an appointment for a pedicure tomorrow but am wondering if I should wait until my foot is past this stage. My podiatrist did say I could get a pedicure now but I would feel terrible for my technician who has to deal with this. Ugh. Just wanted to share incase any of you are having this happen and wondering if it is normal. I have to say I was glad to read it is and that it means healing is occurring! Woo hoo!! See less
I'm nearly two months post-op now and the last of the dead skin has finally come off. I'm quite amazed about how smooth the underlying skin is
Baxter Yup, the snakey skin shed is the norm. My foot now has lovely soft fresh skin far nicer than my good foot I was actually thinking of painting my tootsies too, just a little win

to the MAX Cinthia Walden Exit row concern on Southwest flight LGA to STL I was on a flight from LGA to STL Monday evening. I was sitting in the exit row. The row I was in on both sides had each seat taken. The exit row in front of me was completely empty, on both sides. A couple of passengers from our row and other rows volunteered to move to the exit row so it would be manned in case of an emergency. The stewardess said no. I can’t believe that this is safe?

Morrissey The FAA does NOT require anyone to sit in the exit row. That is the reason flight attendants are on the airplane. Speaking of which.. it’s “flight attendant” for this very reason. Safety. “Stewardess” is 50 years outdated.

1. Assigned seats are not a new concept. Other airlines have assigned seats since the dawn of commercial aviation. 2. No one has to be seated in the emergency exit row. That’s why there are safety information cards at every seat. Read them and follow

Speaking of the new SW policy…regarding seats. What have you been experiencing? What is the best choice on booking?
How do you feel about all these new “fees” for seats?
The flight last month I paid for an “upgraded seat”. There were people around me that said they didn’t pay?
The flight I was just I paid for a standard seat (because I at least like to know my seat number). I picked as close to the front without an additional fee. After takeoff I asked the person next to me if he always picked standard. He said he always picks basic. I paid more money than he did but we sat together. Also I noticed lots of empty seats in the front but the stewart said I couldn’t move to one because SW has a new policy to PROTECT those seats unless paid. We were all squeezed 3 in a row with plenty of empty rows.
I am wondering what the truth is since I have been told two different stories.
Can people move to a better seat if the upgraded seats aren’t sold by check in? See less Stefani Rizer
All the flights I’ve been on have not allowed people to move.
Paul L Fisher As for moving, no. People have paid for or earned the other seats and if SW just gives them away, what would be the point of me earning status?
In addition, people in those seats get free drinks and better snacks.
Just think of an airline that has a real first class. They don’t allow people to move into those seats. Alexis Jean Gibson

to the MAX Bill Wilson Frustrating experience today on a flight from Tucson to Las Vegas. We are A list and booked Choice Extra seats. We boarded and put our backpacks in the overhead. My bag is a 42 liter Cotopaxi. On past flights I have been told it is too large to be placed under the seat. On this flight we were told we had to put them under the seat to make room for other passengers bags. I asked why she was making us take out bags down when I could see lots of other smaller bags in the overhead bins and she snarkily said “Sometimes it’s nice to help other people”. Not sure how good or bad her day has been today, but it was an unusually poor customer experience. See less

As a SWA flight attendant I am so sorry to hear about your experience. We all have to follow FAA regulations that all passengers are allowed only 2 carryon items, one large and one small under the seat in front of you. Idk how many items you have brought on this flight, if you had TWO I would say it is your responsibility to make it fit under the seat, if you had only ONE of this backpack I would insist and tell them that you only have one item and you have the right to keep it in the OHB because everyone should have their own bin space to be fair.
If you bring two bags, only one goes overhead. If you have one bag, put it overhead…you are entitled to your leg room! (FA here)

body: Okay so, second day of work. Literally had to leave after 2 1/2 hours because my feet hurt so bad. Limped my way out of the building, and was in so much pain I cried the whole way home. I’m literally 18 and I’ve been dealing with this for a year now. I’ve tried so many things and I honestly don’t know what to do anymore. I’m going to try a new dr a foot and ankle specialist to see if I can find out what is going on and if that doesn’t

Day 9 post op. I had no pain issues in the first few days although I did follow the docs advice with starting medication early and taking it regularly. Day 6, 7 and 8 were the worst . So painful moving from being in bed to getting up and moving from elevation to walking . I think the pain from elevation is from blood flow back to the injury. I am now doing this in stages and it is helping a lot .

Lets talk shoes.
Im now having up change all my shoes because regular runners dont cut it anymore. I find them to be too narrow.
I bought a pair of red Hobibear shoes off amazon. They are wide toe box for bunions. Soo comfortable.
So I bought 2 more pairs strsight from the Hobibear website and they arrived today. Now I have 3 pairs, red, black, and white. I swear by these shoes. Im considering surgery, but that referral will take a long time before I get a surgery date if I dec
comments: Andrew Antiporda SOME TRUTHS ABOUT PLANTAR FASCITIS. 1.There are no definite medicines for PF. They may decrease your pain momentarily, but it will recur without proper exercise. 2.Orthotics are only good for calming down pain. You have to get rid of them if you seek for permanent cure. 3. Orthotics only calm you pain, but prolonged use make you feet muscles weak. They are time bombs. 4. Same as Orthotics, steroid injections only calm your pain. Prolonged and regular use could even be detrimental to your tendons. 5. Wrong exercises can worsen your PF. Abrupt withdrawal from exercise can worsen your condition. 6. Over-exercise can worsen your condition too that could bring you back to zero. 7. There is no immediate solution for PF. Patience and consistency are some of the major keys. 8. There is no specific magical diet that can cure your PF. 9. You may start with arched elevated heels shoes at the beginning to reduce pain. However you need to gradually decrease the drop to assure that you are correctly strengthening you foot muscles. 10. Barefoot walking is HELL for PF only at the beginning,. . . but it is your goal. If you can walk barefoot the whole day, that is the only indication that you are completely free of PF. 11. It is ideal to wear wide toe, zero drop shoes once you are almost capable, to assure that you PF will never recur again with the aid of gradual, systematic and progressive exercises. 12. Untreated PF can ruin your achilles tendon, your knee, and even your discs. 13. The best cure manifests only in the Goldilocks Zone. Find it through trial and error. Lastly, NO DOCTOR can really provide you with the ultimate cure for PF. They are viewing your pain from without but only you can see from within. The best person who can attend to your situation is you. See less

For the people saying my feet are weak, I was following doctor’s orders. And I think no pain is everyone’s goal on this page. Where is this info from?
Who are you ? This isn’t true we are all individual

body: How many people have used toe spacers and felt relief? Aside from having long toes, the front of my foot has always been so smushed that my pinky toe doesn’t even sit straight. I wonder if that’s part of the problem.

I finally got relief from my plantar fasciitis. I tried lots and lots of things. I go to the chiropractor every Tuesday. I brought up to her about my pain and she suggested to try the activator. We've been using it on my hammer toe and the pain on that is gone and it's just about straight again without surgery. So she did about 10 clicks on the bottom of my foot and heal and I'm not kidding within 2 days I was 90% better. So after 3 times doing this the pain is gone.

I bought some of these a few months ago - and for this price, you have nothing to lose to try it! It's 0.15 (cents) and I'll add the code to get it down to 0.11 (cents). Now I just have to remember wear mine!

Hello. For last couple weeks I have been having pain under/more towards bottom top left foot third toe while walking. I went to a Podiatrist and took X-Rays which were of no concern. I was recommended to wear Zentoes Gel Top Cap but it hardly helps. Not sure if I have to give it few days for the product to work?
Podiatrist wants to see me in 3 weeks and I was recommended ‘Flexor Tenotomy’ procedure which is considered minimally invasi

Surgery was today. Plantar fasciitis, heel spur, hammer toe and Tarsal Tunnel release. As with everyone here, I had tried everything and the pain was constant. I am on top of my pain meds and this is the first time IN TWO YEARS that my feet do not hurt, timgle, or throb.
What a relief!
Interestingly, enough, varicose veins had wrapped themselves around the tarsal tunnel, and they were strangling the ligaments. ￼

body:
Long and possible crazy sounding post warning…
So just curious of others thoughts. 3 years ago my foot pain started in both feet. 1st podiatrist right away said plantar fasciitis and did the usual conservative treatments. It got worse, i even heard a pop in each foot one day while going down some stairs rather quickly. The doctor did 2 rounds of cortisone shots to no avail. X-rays and an MRI according to him didn’t show any tears. T
Just want to share that the only shoes I have found so far that do not hurt my surgical foot are Skechers that have the Goga max foot bed.. they seem to be the most cushioned for me.

Both my ankles and feet are swollen.
Both my ankles are feet are swollen and they hurt so bad sometimes that I can hardly walk; they have been this way for about a couple weeks. The left one hurts slightly more than the right. I have plantar fasciitis and flat feet. I’ve tried the RICE (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation) method. I got a pedicure about a couple weeks ago, hence the pink nails. The massage and soak helped the pain temporarily, but the pain eventually came back.
I

body: Foot pain specialists are the unsung heroes of humanity. They fight bunions, plantar fasciitis, and your terrible shoe choices. Armed with orthotics and stretches, they perform miracles daily. Their enemies? Flip-flops, high heels, and your stubborn refusal to listen. Every day, they see feet that belong in horror movies. But instead of screaming, they get to work, saving soles and sanity. They’ll tell you to stretch, ice, and stop Go

Hello everyone,
Podiatry clinic owner here Stepwise Podiatry AZ
I treat toenail fungus and just wanted to share a few pieces of educational advice that might help troubleshoot the situation:

Hi ....I have ocd contamination and toenail fungus. It's so hard to have the conditions together. I tried to console my mind and looked online and found out about dermophytes. The fungi that can can cause toenail fungus. It has made me maybe even feel worse. These dermophytes if you have toenail fungus can be in your home...maybe in dust too. You can look online. I'm a senior and I isolate myself. Any thoughts? Its hard enou

I tried everything, stretching,rolling,massaging,castor oil,shoe inserts ,higher arches in shoes, referal to physio, many types of different shoes... and then i suddenly read somewhere that shoe arches and cushioned insoles could be making the problem worse. I saw my podiatrist on Monday. I am allowed full use of my foot now, with a shoe on, and found out something I wanted to share with you all.
The reason the doctors have you putting weight on your foot so early, even with all the swelling that comes with it etc, is to prevent disuse osteopenia. It is where the bone minerals start to break down because your body tells your foot it isn't being used so no need to rebuild bone.
How did I find this out? Because my surgeon's PA

to the MAX Kathy Clark Southwest finally did it. We have been exclusively loyal to SW for over 20 years. We started branching out about 4 years ago, because the SW schedule either didn't go to places we wanted to go, or made it more difficult to get there. (ex. Reno used to be nonstop. It hasn't been for a while. Times for nonstop flights to some of our favorite places have become very inconvenient.) Anyway.....even though it is much easier for us to get to DFW than to Love Field, we made the trek, because we LOVED SW. Even though SW hasn't been a "low cost carrier" for quite some time, we tried to hang on through all the changes, believing in the company's stated ethos. Our last experience has possibly finally torn it. We were traveling to Denver. I purchased my ticket, got a CP seat for my husband, and paid for seat upgrades for both of us. All good. We needed to cancel the trip. My ticket $ and the $ for seats upgrades were available for another ticket we purchased, including the seat upgrade. My CP's $ for seat upgrades was only available for ticket costs (which, thankfully, includes the $11.20 for fees). It is NOT available to pay for seat upgrades. Hmmmmm.......after a lengthy convo w/a lovely person at SW, she explained that this was the policy now. That was one change I was not aware of, While I've been able to swallow most of the changes that have happened, even when they seemed ridiculous (CP upgrade price being less than mine, upgrade prices being cheaper in the app or variable by day, flight credits expiring in 6 months, when you buy the basic seat, etc.), it seems almost like robbery to use good $ to pay for a CP seat upgrade in good faith, but not have it available to pay for CP upgrade for a new ticket. We now have a balance for him that will take more flights to use, that expires before we will use it. That's not right. I think that finally made the decision for us. Sad day in our household. See less

I’m curious as to which airline you think will give you a better deal? Most airlines don’t have companion pass at all. I am basically loyal to whichever airline gives me the best deal and has reliable service. So far, for me, that has been Southwest.

For those of you trying to use Claude assistants in your agency, this post is for you. Giving my team this tool might be the most dramatic thing I've ever done for our efficiency, and we're only getting faster. I know there is a learning curve, so hopefully this helps those of you who are interested wrap your heads around it.
First and foremost, you should set up an Organization account. This allows you, as the admin, to control connectors and skills running on each of your team's computers. You can also set guardrails and such here as well to keep data safe.
Once you've done that, you'll be able to set up your connectors and skills. Connectors are basically secure connections to MCP servers for certain apps. Skills are a set of instructions that tell the AI how to respond to certain prompts.
By setting it up this way, your skills and connectors can work in unison for your team. Think of it like this;
Every skill can use every skill and connector, and every connector can feed data back and forth with every skill.
So, for example(s);
Prompt: Generate a proposal for these quotes [pdf attached] and send it to John Smith.
The AI can then handle this from cover to cover using skills. It starts by pinging the Proposal Generator skill for its instructions on how to generate a proposal, then the brand kit for colors and logos, then Hubspot to collect the user's email address, and then Gmail to draft the email with the proposal to the client.
Prompt: Generate a flood insurance quote for 123 ABC Lane.
The AI takes the address and looks at the Flood skill. The flood skills tell it what variables it needs to do a quote. It then uses the property data skill to collect that info, then returns to the flood skill to generate a quote.
Prompt: Requote John Smiths home and auto
The AI looks at our Comp Rater skill for the variables it needs. Once it knows, it hits HubSpot and pulls the client and policy data it needs. It then uses those variables, hits our rating skill and returns rates.
The basic idea is you can built out a suite of AI agents (skills) that can complete complex tasks in your agency.
What are y'all building with Claude right now? See less

comments: Pelotonia training is big in Granville. Especially on the roads with the most hills and climbing. They are riding to End Cancer. Please be patient and share the road. Cars lawfully need to slow down and give 3 feet of clearance.

Sobjack The “bike path” is a multi-use trail and it has a speed limit. It is not safe if you are riding a road bike at an adult speed. I ride with my kid on the bike path, and I am grateful for the road bikers who use the road so that the path is safe for children! I also ride myself on the road. Loudon and Raccoon Valley is a popular route. Groups ride from New Albany and Columbus to come out here because it is beautiful, good hills, and the drivers here are usually kind about sharing the road. I appreciate the drivers who look out and help keep us safe!

Field Services LLC is in Milford. Field Notes | Vol. III Showed up to look at a porch column wrap. During the walkaround, the driveway told its own story. Alligator cracking, a pothole opened down to the base, grass growing through the seams, and edges starting to break apart. Curb appeal doesn’t stop at the front door. The driveway is one of the first things people see when they pull up. Once water gets into cracks like this, every storm and freeze cycle keeps working it open. What starts as a few rough spots can turn into a much bigger repair or replacement conversation if it gets ignored too long. This is why a full property walk matters. For driveways starting to crack, fade, open up, or grow grass through the seams, TSFS handles crack repair, small patch areas, edge cleanup, and seal coating as part of our property maintenance services. body:
To "Ryan" of Sarasota who has an Airbnb in Venice, THANK YOU for saving us from hosting a nitemare guest!
Somehow our "Requires Good Track record" button was toggled OFF on our Instant Booking option and "Inna" snuck in! I read Ryan's review of her which was one of the 2 negative reviews of her 3 stay Airbnb career and was horrified to see that we were potentially her next victim! Called Airbnb customer service who were actually very accomodating in helping me quickly cancel the booking with no penalty and alerted me of the instant booking setting; which i quickly toggeled BACK on!
Moral of the story; check your Instant Booking setings!
Thanks again, Ryan!
See less
comments: My "requires good track record" keeps getting turned off too!!!! No idea how it is happening, now I check it every few days.
Airbnb was wonderful when I had the same experience. Airbnb has always given me great customer service, thankfully, I don't need it often.

body:
I wanted to share a very rare but very recent experience we had with a horrible guest. I have attached screenshots of my review of her, and below is her message back to me via the platform.
Her response shows that some people will never take personal responsibility and will always blame everybody/anybody else for their poor behavior.
It's unlikely that we'll get a dime from her to cover damages but we're more concerned about warning the next host.
Accurate guest reviews are so important to hosts!
Alhana's response
"I'm not sure where all of this came from.. my daughter asked that her one friend came with us. And yes, I should have updated that in the system. My dog was let out numerous time out the back door off the ring camera and was properly picked up after. As far as cleaning, floors were swept and mopped. all dishes were cleaned except for one glass that flew on my daughter's head from the top shelf from simply opening the cabinet without anyone touching it leaving her with 3 stitches . And all towels were washed. Yes my girlfriend has red hair and it died the towels and the kids had glitter but we did clean behind us. As far as the support animal being unattended, she was contained on the back porch with food and water while we took the kids to the pool. Would you have loved me to take the dog to go swimming too?? She a support animal that's here to help with my kids and their emotional and mental needs not be one of my kids that I should take swimming. The mentioning of smoke concerns me as we never smoked in the house. I have a lock box with my medical dispensary stuff in it but even cigarettes were smoked in the truck that you can see us walking out to smoke out of the house. As far as the disturbances at that time, is when we checked in coming from out of town late waiting for kids to get out of school so I apologize our travel time was not convenient to you and I didn't realize my family and myself were now being micro managed to the point that a grown woman am now being targeted as a child. I'll be sure to send some people your way to have stripper parties since a family get away isn't your style. Again I apologize my life has been such an awful inconvenience to you. Maybe you should make your place kid friendly and learn how to put dishes away the right way so more kids don't get hurt due to your neglect." See less
comments: Why would you do that? What did you hope to gain other than an angry customer and negative attention? How does that benefit your business. As my Mother always said…. “If you have nothing nice to say, say nothing.” smdh

But I’ve never encountered any of that nonsense. Glitter??? Cmon man. Everybody knows that stuff is a nightmare and ya bring it into a rental?! UG! At least take it outside. Hi! I’m new here and hoping to learn more about what helped people. I have an autoimmune disease that causes inflammation etc and is a huge factor of my PF flare ups. This disease tends to attack and focus on my “weak spots” and obviously for me this is one of my MAJOR issues. It comes out of nowhere, when my autoimmune is flaring usually my feet are the first to face the brunt of it. That being said, I’m focused on inflammation reduction. I am on an anti inflammatory diet, I’m active and I work out 3-5 times a week. HOWEVER this has been kicking my butt and I’m in constant pain. It makes it extremely difficult to walk etc. the podiatrist is not very helpful but I do start PT next week. In the mean time, is there something on Amazon I can buy? A brace of some sort? I wear Hokusai usually but that’s not enough at this point. See less
comments: Hi, I recently asked about night splints in a post. Go to the top right search icon and type in night splint and see if my post pops up . Some good information. People say they’re helpful. I’ve been dealing w this for almost a year now and that’s one thing I Haven’t tried . Also I recommend just scrolling through posts and you’ll come across good information and things to try. Unfortunately there isn’t a one size fits all cure for PF. We’ve all tried many different things. This group will be helpful. Hope you feel better soon

Autoimmune conditions can be so frustrating and unpredictable. Has anyone checked your feet for adhesions and scar tissue? They keep the areas of the feet weak and muscles, connective tissue, and nerves don’t move as they should because they’re bound up. Google these in your area: structural integrated massage, Airrosti, active release technique, and Rolfing. Active release technique would probably be the easiest to locate. Therapist may also use graston/IASTM to assist.

have questions? Get Answers! Gwen Taraya I just got the MIB surgery yesterday and I am home now. My bunion progressed so bad that my big toe is touching the middle toe and the 2nd toe sitting on top, spacers help relieve but to some extent only. All went well and the medical team is fantastic! Im now focusing on recovery. I was asleep during the procedure (general anaesthesia) and was given nerve block. Surgery commenced 2:30pm. I was woken up at 4:20pm in recovery room to tell me it was all done. I was oblivious! I was freaking out about the surgery for no reason, reading too much internet will play tricks in your mind. Its almost 24 hrs post op and I can weight bear as tolerated with some crutches. Im getting on top of pain management strictly following dr’s orders. My BP drops so I am light headed, i needed to be supervised when going to toilet to avoid falls. I am a triathlete and running with bunion is a torture. My coach who is now in his 70’s had MIBS in 2024 to both his feet, he’s back training in just a couple of months post op (he just finished another ironman this year). I am permitted to go back swim training once my wounds are healed, and then slowly progressing back to cycling (unclipping bike shoes will be a challenge) then last get back to running. Hopefully my recovery goes well See less
Quinn I had mine done on Wednesday! Here’s to a quick recovery and back to running soon
Egolf I had a good experience too. I wish I would have done it a long time ago. The first week little rough but after it is worth it.

body:
Why JW Landscape Lighting, LLC Became More Than Lights
My name is Jim Wielgus, owner of JW Landscape Lighting, LLC.
When I first started my company, my focus was helping homeowners improve and beautify their properties through professional landscape lighting. As I spent more time meeting customers and working on their homes, I noticed something important. Many homeowners needed help with much more than lighting.
Every day, I was hearing the same things:
“Do you know someone who can fix this?”
“Can you help with this project?”
“I’ve been meaning to get this done but don’t know who to call.”
I realized there was a real need in our community for a trustworthy, reliable handyman service. Many homeowners, especially seniors, don’t always know what maintenance items need attention or who they can trust to do the work correctly. Others simply don’t have the time, tools, or physical ability to complete these projects themselves.
With my background in construction and home improvement, including experience helping build homes from the foundation to the finishing touches, I knew I could help. That’s why JW Landscape Lighting, LLC has expanded beyond lighting services to become a trusted handyman company serving our community.
What makes us different is that I personally meet with customers to understand their needs. I don’t believe in a one-size-fits-all approach. Every home and every homeowner is different. By listening to our customers, we can provide solutions that improve the safety, appearance, and value of their homes.
We also believe in educating homeowners. Many of the services we advertise come from real situations we’ve encountered while working in homes throughout Northeast Ohio. We share maintenance tips and information because we want homeowners to be aware of things that can help protect and maintain their investment.
At JW Landscape Lighting, LLC, our priorities are simple:
• Honesty
• Reliability
• Trustworthiness
• Quality workmanship
• Outstanding customer service
We are proud to be “More Than Lights.” We are committed to helping homeowners with the projects that make their homes safer, more comfortable, and better maintained.
Thank you for trusting JW Landscape Lighting, LLC. We look forward to continuing to serve our community for years to come. See less
JW Landscape Lighting

Have a starwars home model pinball where the ribbon cable got snapped off unfortunately. Looking for a new LCD replacment, i have called stern they said email parts but its been a few days anyone know where I could source this? Brutuss Yager
Just be patient and wait for stern to send you the part man. You ain’t gonna source it anywhere else.
Sterns support is generally pretty good and if you send them pics of the issue and they said they’re gonna send you the part then they will.
But that doesn’t happen in just 48 hours man lol
they have to contact the parts dept and source the part and send the paperwork to order you a new one. That process can take a week or two. You have to be patient dude. But Keep calling like every 4 days till they can give you an actual tracking number for the part tho. After a week or so they should be able to give you a tracking number for the part.

Answer as Justin STERN PINBALL ENTHUSIASTS Kevin Oatsvall Top contributor This is more for newbies but when looking at machines for purchase, the number of plays doesn't matter as much as how well it has been maintained & cleaned. Yes number of plays will alert you if it has been on location or home use only but really the lifetime plays really only comes into consideration with newer machines where the play count can't be reset. Older machines could easily be reset because of how the electronics were when batteries were needed to retain memory. There's no telling how many plays my refurbished Tales from the Crypt has had over the years. I will never know. What I do know is that it plays awesome and feels like a new machine. Everything on a pinball machine is pretty much easily replaceable to make new again. So knowing what to look for is key and how it plays is most important when purchasing a used machine. My Ghostbusters for example has 21k plays and has been to Canada & back but you would never know it. It's in awesome condition and plays like new. I've heard some distributors state that number of plays doesn't matter but they want to see your shooter lane if you're trading a game which makes no sense to me. If a game has 30k plays of course it's going to have a little wear in the shooter lane. So the shooter lane shouldn't matter either. And you can always repair a shooter lane too if it has a lot of damage. On another note, if the game has the option of lowering the trough power then that's highly recommended to cut down on shooter lane damage. See less All reactions:
Comment Michael Beza Rising contributor How do you repair a shooting lane?
View 1 reply Jason Smith I added a shooter lane protector to my first and only (so far) machine to avoid that issue.
View 1 reply Answer as Justin

Answer as Justin STERN PINBALL ENTHUSIASTS Kevin Oatsvall Top contributor This is more for newbies but when looking at machines for purchase, the number of plays doesn't matter as much as how well it has been maintained & cleaned. Yes number of plays will alert you if it has been on location or home use only but really the lifetime plays really only comes into consideration with newer machines where the play count can't be reset. Older machines could easily be reset because of how the electronics were when batteries were needed to retain memory. There's no telling how many plays my refurbished Tales from the Crypt has had over the years. I will never know. What I do know is that it plays awesome and feels like a new machine. Everything on a pinball machine is pretty much easily replaceable to make new again. So knowing what to look for is key and how it plays is most important when purchasing a used machine. My Ghostbusters for example has 21k plays and has been to Canada & back but you would never know it. It's in awesome condition and plays like new. I've heard some distributors state that number of plays doesn't matter but they want to see your shooter lane if you're trading a game which makes no sense to me. If a game has 30k plays of course it's going to have a little wear in the shooter lane. So the shooter lane shouldn't matter either. And you can always repair a shooter lane too if it has a lot of damage. On another note, if the game has the option of lowering the trough power then that's highly recommended to cut down on shooter lane damage. See less All reactions:

Has anyone been to see a podiatrist and have they given you exercises and hot and cold treatment suggestions? I have been doing this religiously because the pain has been immense. It has actually worked a little enough for me to function better.
***always check with your physician pain management team or GP first before trying***
Sitting down, feet flat on the floor, and gently roll your feet forwards heel up, and then roll your foot back heel down and toes up towards the ceiling. My pain is caused by my back issues. I do it sitting on my bed, the toilet, settee anywhere I can.
He also gave me heel raise supports and even though they hurt my back it is helping my foot pain.
I use a foot spa for the warmth and take my feet out and out the cold compress on feet for like 30 seconds then put them back on the warm water. It tingles really bad at first but stick with it, it stops (do not exceed your pain threshold if it hurts too much take your feet out of the water immediately) and Picture is also the cold compress I got from Amazon.
Just wanted to share these with you if it helps. Just do it gently and carefully. DO NOT HOT WATER USE WARM WATER!!!

comments: View more comments Ann Shepard Don't let this get you down! I know - I remember hobbling. It's awful but evaluate what you are doing now and decide what you think could be working even a little bit and keep it up. Add some new things. You might consider- I know it is controversial- a steroid shot(stay off your foot as much as possible for 48 hours) for some relief. I've also heard of wearing a boot for a time period- ask your doctor if this will work for your specific situation. I dealt with it for over 2 years and have a job that requires standing . A year later, I was struck with a virus in both of my eyes that was extremely serious, continues to be, and affects my life daily. I've had multiple operations. My point is don't let this get to you- get a boot on if that works for you and exercise as much you can. PF takes a lot of patience and grit.

StunningDachshund3361 I’m sorry, I understand how you must feel. For me, even not being able to wear shoes or cute sandals anymore and yes I know there are cute supportive sandals and stuff out there but it’s like a whole process to determine a good fit and then they’re usually expensive. . So what goes good with my boring black shoes today I guess I took for granted being able to walk / run feel good last year and then I turned 37 and it hit me .

Someone put a spy camera inside my TV and the guests called the cops.
By the time I arrived, the cops were already dismantling the TV. When they removed the back panel there was a lot of space enough to put a USB power supply, a tiny transmitter, antenna and a tiny camera poking right above the remote control receiver. The guests saw blinking blue light.
In shock -- cops said they will be back and I should not enter that property. The guests then left.
Since I couldn't sleep, I looked up ways to prevent it. If the internet company allows you to have your own DHCP server, then buy an expensive router that lets you upload and backup all DHCP logs. Then you can run custom scripts to see if a new device is constantly on your network. Guests typically stay only for a few days. So what is this new device? In my case, the dhcp server is on the modem given to me by the internet company and I cannot configure anything. Cops will have to contact the Internet company and find out if they can tell us when this this supply device first came online.
On the 25th of November last year a guest complained that the TV fell off because it was held by one screw. This was obviously a lie because I had four screws on stud and there's no way a lightweight modern TV which probably weighs 15 lb will fall off. I think he took it down. Put the camera in. But could not put it back at the original spot because it was very high up. So he put it up down low and spun up the light to cover his tracks. If he had been able to put it at the original spot he would not have messaged me.
Another solution is installing two networks . One regular network for smart devices, the network is hidden and password is not known to the guests. The second access point like Flint, running a custom captive portal where the user has to enter his email ID. And they got kicked out after a few days. That way I also get to have their email IDs. Also if you leave the camera etc. Only router hidden guests can never disable your cameras See less

When they removed the back panel there was a lot of space enough to put a USB power supply, a tiny transmitter, antenna and a tiny camera poking right above the remote control receiver. The guests saw blinking blue light. In shock -- cops said they will be back and I should not enter that property. The guests then left. Since I couldn't sleep, I looked up ways to prevent it. If the internet company allows you to have your own DHCP server, then buy an expensive router that lets you upload and backup all DHCP logs. Then you can run custom scripts to see if a new device is constantly on your network. Guests typically stay only for a few days. So what is this new device? In my case, the dhcp server is on the modem given to me by the internet company and I cannot configure anything. Cops will have to contact the Internet company and find out if they can tell us when this this supply device first came online. On the 25th of November last year a guest complained that the TV fell off because it was held by one screw. This was obviously a lie because I had four screws on stud and there's no way a lightweight modern TV which probably weighs 15 lb will fall off. I think he took it down. Put the camera in. But could not put it back at the original spot because it was very high up. So he put it up down low and spun up the light to cover his tracks. If he had been able to put it at the original spot he would not have messaged me. Another solution is installing two networks . One regular network for smart devices, the network is hidden and password is not known to the guests. The second access point like Flint, running a custom captive portal where the user has to enter his email ID. And they got kicked out after a few days. That way I also get to have their email IDs. Also if you leave the camera etc. Only router hidden guests can never disable your cameras See less All reactions:

My wife and I did the Stern factory tour yesterday. Transformers Pros and a few LEs were on the line.  It was really cool to see how these machines are built from start to finish. The Stern team was great and the tour is something I highly recommend for any pinball enthusiasts.
We love pinball and our kids are huge Transformers fans so we were going to  buy our first LE machine—despite the decision to pull transforming Optimus at the 11th hour. Because this is such a big purchase for us we wanted to see and play it before pulling the trigger, which is why we made the trip to Chicago and scheduled the tour at this time.
During the tour we were told they had two Transformers machines in the showroom but one had just been sent off—I assume to the NW Pinball and Arcade Show—so there was just one machine to play. Well to the tour guide and our surprise, the second machine had been removed too.  We did not get to see or play a finished product. I think this was a miss on Stern’s part. If you have a new release you are promoting you should make sure the showroom model stays in the showroom. Had we got to see it I would likely have been online with my distributor paying my deposit before we left the factory.  Now we will be waiting until we can see an LE or Premium on location to decide. Of course maybe it is a blessing in disguise with JJP’s Sonic about to be released. I only have money and room for one.
A final note, it was really refreshing to see how Stern cares about their employees. Working in a factory is not an easy job.  But it was obvious Stern goes the extra mile to make sure their employees have a great work environment and know they are part of a team that brings joy to all of us in the pinball community. See less
